review_comments:
Only this line is edited manually, others are changed automatically by `python scripts/docs.py build-all
`.
# 代替ツールから受けた影響と比較
何が**FastAPI**に影響を与えたのか、他の代替ツールと比較してどうか、そしてそこから何を学んだのかについて。
ある時点で、 -> その時点では、
**FastAPI**は、代替ツールのこれまでの働きがなければ存在しなかったでしょう。
バックエンドでHTMLを生成するために作られたものであり、現代的なフロントエンド（ReactやVue.js、Angularなど）や、他のシステム（<abbr title="Internet of Things">IoT</abbr>デバイスなど）と通信するためのAPIを構築するために作られたものではありません。
確かにそうですね。「影響」は少しニュアンスが違うような気がしますので、「インスピレーション」に統一しようと思います。
ありがとうございます。「半角スペース + 半角括弧」で統一します。
!!! note "備考"
!!! info "情報"
!!! info "情報"
!!! info "情報"
!!! note "技術詳細"

```suggestion
バックエンドでHTMLを生成するために作られたものであり、現代的なフロントエンド (ReactやVue.js、Angularなど) や、他のシステム (IoTデバイスなど) と通信するAPIを構築するために作られたものではありません。
```

「ため」が連続していて、日本語として少し違和感を感じました。

```suggestion
Django REST Frameworkは、Djangoを下敷きにしてWeb APIを構築する柔軟なツールキットとして、APIの機能を向上させるために作られました。
```

「ため」が連続していて、日本語として少し違和感を感じました。

```suggestion
APIシステムで必要とされる主な機能の一つに、コード (Python) からデータを取り出して、ネットワークを介して送れるものに変換するデータの「<abbr title="marshalling, conversion">シリアライゼーション</abbr>」があります。例えば、データベースのデータを含むオブジェクトをJSONオブジェクトに変換したり、`datetime` オブジェクトを文字列に変換するなどです。
```

冗長に感じました。ない方が読みやすい気がします。

```suggestion
同じフレームワークを使ってAPIとCLIを作成できる、面白く珍しい機能を持っています。
```
冗長に感じました。ない方が読みやすい気がします。

title: Feature/pagination
body:
this is the feature that supports pagination, 
with the param with_page_split as True, the method will calculate the length of result, split the result by page_num and page_size params and finally return previous url and next_url
pyandtic.BaseModel, sqlalchemy object and normal type are supported

title: Feature/pagination
body:
this is the feature that supports pagination, 
with the param with_page_split as True, the method will calculate the length of result, split the result by page_num and page_size params and finally return previous url and next_url
pyandtic.BaseModel, sqlalchemy object and normal type are supported

This section is technical, descriptive and long. I translated loosely to make it easier to read for Japanese.

title: 🐛 Prefer custom encoder over defaults if specified in `jsonable_encoder`
body:
In `encoders.py/jsonable_encoder`, default encoders for each type are specified first & are used in preference to custom encoders. 

This affects custom encoders specified for certain types like enums such that the default encoder is always executed and any custom encoders specified are ignored. See - https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1986 - for a description of this issue and example code as well that illustrates the problem.

This PR adds a change to prefer custom encoders if specified in preference to default encoders.

Disclaimer - I am completely new to this code base :-)

> Hello! This does appear to fix my issue at #2091. However, if I may make one suggestion to this PR, please also fix the mutable dictionary that is set as a keyword argument with this code:
> 
> ```python
>     custom_encoder: dict = None,     # <------- Make this change
>     sqlalchemy_safe: bool = True,
> ) -> Any:
>     custom_encoder = custom_encoder or {}       # <-------- And this change
>     if custom_encoder:
>         if type(obj) in custom_encoder:
>             return custom_encoder[type(obj)](obj)
>         else:
>             for encoder_type, encoder in custom_encoder.items():
>                 if isinstance(obj, encoder_type):
>                     return encoder(obj)
> ```
> 
> More details on this is here: https://docs.python-guide.org/writing/gotchas/#mutable-default-arguments

I agree! I can make this change if there are no objections.

Question: does this cover the use case of CSV-like quoted strings: `GET /items?foo="a,bc",d` (example taken from [here](https://specs.openstack.org/openstack/api-wg/guidelines/pagination_filter_sort.html#filtering)). A simple `.split` would result in `['"a', 'bc"', 'd']` which means no parameter values could have delimiters in them.

@adriangb 
You're right - it won't handle such use case. I think the easiest way to handle it is to reuse built-in `csv` python module and replace:

```python
value = value.split(delimiter)
```
with
```python
value = list(csv.reader([value], delimiter=delimiter))[0]
```

The only problem I can see is that we can go a step further and ask: what about quotes in quotes? Actually, this is handled by `csv` module as well but there are different strategies available and AFAIK, OpenAPI does not specify which one should be used:

```python
import csv
from typing import List, Optional

def parse_query_field(value: str, doublequote: bool = True, escapechar: Optional[str] = None) -> List[str]:
   return list(csv.reader([value], delimiter=',', doublequote=doublequote, escapechar=escapechar))[0]

EXPECTED = ['a', '"b,c,d"', 'e,f']

# default behaviour of csv module
assert(EXPECTED == parse_query_field('a,"""b,c,d""","e,f"', doublequote=True))

# example from the website you linked where escape chars are used
assert(EXPECTED == parse_query_field('a,"\\"b,c,d\\"","e,f"', doublequote=False, escapechar='\\'))
```

Ideally, it should be up to the FastAPI user to define it (more parameters?) but I'm not sure we want to go that way. Anyway, I rebased my changes on the top of current master and added simple fix with `csv` module with default values.

@tiangolo did you have a change to look at this?
📝 Docs preview for commit 9a5fc6943c779483405c893213778b92ca77205c at: https://6012a173dd01fac2e1bfb714--fastapi.netlify.app
@tiangolo Sorry for pinging, any chance this will be merged any time soon?

review_comments: What is the point of nested if statement here?

I assume you're suggesting to merge these two conditions like this?
```python
if isinstance(field_info, Query) and not field_info.explode:
   parameter["explode"] = field_info.explode
   parameter["style"] = field_info.style.value
```

I can't see a reason why I chose nested version over this one. Maybe it was left after some refactoring. I can change that and update the branch as there are some conflicts with master.

> I assume you're suggesting to merge these two conditions like this?

Yes
You can also use [the walrus operator](https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.8.html#assignment-expressions) to merge this condition as well if the python version is compatible.
That's true but AFAIK FastAPI tries to be compatible with python 3.6+ so I guess walrus is out of the question.

title: Add option for custom validator for Path params.
body:
Nowadays, the only supported validation for Path params are the given ones (le, ge, etc).
I think there are many use cases where we want to sanitize and validate input of Path parameters that is "smarter" than the given options.
I will add documentation once I get approval of concept :)

title: 📝 Simplify intro to Python Types, all currently supported Python versions include type hints 🎉
body:
Function annotation syntax was proposed in [pep 3107](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3107/) in 2006 and was a feature of Python 3.0. Type annotations were already a proposed usecase at this time. Mypy supports Python 3.2 and newer. A standard vocabulary for type hints (and the typing module itself) based on conventions used in Mypy and other sources was added in 3.5 with [pep 484](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0484/).

The only thing new for type hints in 3.6 is _variable annotations_, which allows types to be specified outside of function signatures. [pep 526](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0526/)

I think this proposal is *technically* correct but I find some drawbacks.

1. Typing module was provisional in 3.5. It become stable in either 3.6.4 or 3.7.
2. Pydantic uses 3.6 in it's documentation and it has way more weight in on types.
3. Some people might read it as 'fastapi can run on python 3.5' which might be possible but is not suggested at all.

Is it possible to rephrase this in such a way that it is both technically correct and doesn't give people the wrong idea? (a possible option would be to omit details about when type annotations were introduced altogether)
Considering 3.5 reached end of life this month, completely removing the version sounds the best.
📝 Docs preview for commit a9589a0943817a12500c32717a466b5968a80ff7 at: https://5f6ce1c73b57e61ca550f17c--fastapi.netlify.app

Awesome, thanks! It's so nice that type hints are now "mainstream" and standard in all the Python versions supported! :tada: 

Thanks for your contribution! :rocket: :tada: 

And thanks @ArcLightSlavik for those points :heavy_check_mark: :mag:

title: Add Stale bot configuration
body:
The configuration is basically the same like the example config of https://probot.github.io/apps/stale/.
Differences:
- 90 instead of 60 days: for the start of the bot we should allow issues to be stale a little bit longer. This can be decrease in the future.
- exemptLabels:
  - pinned and security: are not part of the project, but could be useful in the future.
  - help-wanted: is probably something which the authors are willing to work on.
- markComment: make it more clear what "recent" means

Fixes #2049.
Additionally stale bot has to be installed into the repository. This can only be done by the maintainers.

Sorry if stale bot is not something which is wanted for this project. I probably should've checked with the maintainers before.

I think this needs to be thought about a bit more - there are other mimetypes that are JSON but which don't use the application/json mimetype. application/hal+json is one such common example of this

Good point, @Mause. Based on [this list](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml) of registered mimetypes, it seems safe to interpret as JSON any mimetype that _ends_ with "json". Notably, this excludes the "json-seq" type, which is not itself a subset of JSON.

How do we like conflicts to be handled? I can rebase and force-push to my feature branch...
review_comments: Is Content-Type not being there a concern? I believe RFC says it should always be filled.
Would be considered a breaking change. Think even my work API has a test similar to this.
[RFC 7231](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-3.1.1.5) suggests it is not strictly required, and that we should assume "application/octet-stream" if it's not present.

title: 🐛 Fix exception status codes in security http schemes
body:
Hi!

**_TL;DR:_**
This PR changes exception response status codes in security http schemes from 403_FORBIDDEN to 401_UNAUTHORIZED.

**__Long__**
HTTPBearer and HTTPDigest security schemes used as a dependency are returning a 403_FORBIDDEN status on scheme Exception. This should be 401_UNAUTHORIZED status because we can't identify user without token or with invalid credentials.



There's issue mentioning this: #2026
comments:

📝 Docs preview for commit cf9a1e27e54e412244217f20291d6994073aaf4a at: https://5f7751fc8bcb68537c8b9835--fastapi.netlify.app

I was just about to open a similar PR myself, and noticed yours here :smile: 

In general, the status code for invalid authorization seems to depend on the application. The use case I am building would require 401 for a missing (or malformed, e.g. not `/Bearer [a-z0-9]+/i`)  Authorization header.

But I have seen other status codes being used.

**400**: If header was malformed. In my opinion, this is bad form. But I've seen it, to make client-side error handling easier; any request parameter - be it header, body, query, whatever - must match _this format_, and if not, 400 is sent. 400 is then handled client side as something like `Your request did not match the specification blablabla`

**403**: For example if an auth header was provided, but malformed, you may wish to respond _as if_ it was valid auth, but insufficient scope. This also matches the [RFC](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.3), in my opinion:

> If authentication credentials were provided in the request, the server considers them insufficient to grant access.  The client SHOULD NOT automatically repeat the request with the same credentials.  The client MAY repeat the request with new or different credentials.  However, a request might be forbidden for reasons unrelated to the credentials.

**404**: E.g. if you wish to obfuscate the endpoint.

This was a long winded comment, in order to justify the following: I think the constructor should accept a parameter `status_code`, which defaults to 401.

I'd like to add my support for this PR.

In response to @aksel:
>403: For example if an auth header was provided, but malformed, you may wish to respond as if it was valid auth, but insufficient scope. This also matches the RFC, in my opinion

It is true that the quoted definition of 403 fits the scenario you describe. However, the definition of 401 in the same [RFC](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1) fits it _better_:
> The 401 (Unauthorized) status code indicates that the request has not
   been applied because it lacks valid authentication credentials for
   the target resource.

> However, the definition of 401 in the same [RFC](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1) fits it _better_

I agree.
@gostekk Thanks for putting this change together. Are there plans to merge / release this soon?
Bumping this. Would be great to get this merged.
Also stopping by to support this change! I had conversations about this status code thing this week with colleagues, it would be good to see this get merged. =)
Would love to see this fixed. Any update on this?

Hi @tiangolo! Sorry to tag you but just doing it in case you missed this one, since this PR is supported by a few people already.
Thanks!

```suggestion
* `allow_methods` - オリジン間リクエストで許可するHTTPメソッドのリスト。デフォルトは `['GET']` です。`['*']`を使用してすべての標準メソッドを許可できます。
```

Remove extra space.

```suggestion
そして、ブラウザ上で実行されているフロントエンド (`http://localhost:8080`) があり、そのJavaScriptが `http://localhost` で実行されているバックエンドと通信するとします。(ポートを指定していないので、ブラウザはデフォルトの`80`ポートを使用します)
```

I think that's a bit hard to read. How about this?

```suggestion
`CORSMiddleware` 実装のデフォルトのパラメータはCORSに関して制限を与えるものになっているので、ブラウザにドメインを跨いで特定のオリジン、メソッド、またはヘッダーを使用可能にするためには、それらを明示的に有効にする必要があります
```

```suggestion
この場合、フロントエンドを正しく機能させるには、そのリストに `http://localhost:8080` を含める必要があります。
```

「なければなりません。」 is consecutive, so another expression would be more natural.

```suggestion
これを実現するには、バックエンドに「許可されたオリジン」のリストがなければなりません。
```

```suggestion
* `allow_headers` - オリジン間リクエストでサポートするHTTPリクエストヘッダーのリスト。デフォルトは `[]` です。`['*']`を使用して、すべてのヘッダーを許可できます。CORSリクエストでは、 `Accept` 、 `Accept-Language` 、 `Content-Language` 、 `Content-Type` ヘッダーが常に許可されます。
```

```suggestion
これらは、 `Origin` ヘッダーと `Access-Control-Request-Method` ヘッダーを持つ `OPTIONS` リクエストです。
```

Translation of "Cookie" should be "Cookie" or "クッキー" ??
I found some of the pages used "クッキー", so I wanted to be consistent with wording.
(https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1933/files)

I prefer "Cookie" than "クッキー", because cookie is common technical term.

hmmm... let us use `cookie` because it is used in browser settings.
I may use both of them in other PR's. I'll check it.

can you list it in main issue?
let us discuss if you find other words.

title: 📝 Add misc dependency installs to tutorial docs
body:
### Overview
Just some small (potential) improvements to the tutorial, adding required dependency installations that are otherwise not mentioned in their corresponding tutorial sections. 

### Reasoning:

Although:

- They are mentioned (with the exception of `SQLAlchemy`) in the documentation home page (`docs/index.md`) under the section "Optional Dependencies"
- The tutorial home page (`docs/tutorial/index.md`) recommends installing FastAPI with all the optional dependencies and features (i.e., `pip install fastapi[all]`)
- Corresponding error messages make it fairly obvious as to what dependencies are missing and need to be installed

These additions may still be helpful for beginners and seem to be in keeping with the style of the documentation as a whole (i.e., standalone sections).

title: 📝 Fix renamed parameter `content_type` typo
body:
The `content_type` parameter was renamed to `response_class` in release `0.19.0`

### Tangential question to maintainers:

This is the 3rd documentation-related PR I've opened this week. They all fall into different categories, but all have fairly small changes. 
- Is this disruptive? 
- Would it be better to condense any near-future PRs into a single "documentation" PR? 

Thanks in advance!

I tried 
```

from fastapi import FastAPI, Form, Depends, UploadFile
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI()


class SimpleModel(BaseModel):
    no: int = Form(...)
    nm: str = Form(...)
    f: UploadFile = Form(...)


@app.post("/form")
async def form_post(form_data: SimpleModel = Depends()):
    fp = await form_data.f.read()
    print(fp)
```

It works with current (0.86) version. Is this PR still needed?

Thanks! :coffee: 

This was covered in https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/12129, it is available in FastAPI 0.113.0 :tada: 

The new docs: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/request-form-models/

Given that, I'll close this one, but thanks for the effort! :cake:

review_comments:
```suggestion
!!! check "Authorizeボタン!"
```

This note refers to the button labeled "Authorize", so I think it's safe to leave it there.

```suggestion
それをクリックすると、`ユーザー名`と`パスワード` (およびその他のオプションフィールド) を入力する小さな認証フォームが表示されます:
```

```suggestion
これをフロントエンドチームが使用できます (また、あなた自身が使用することもできます) 。
```

```suggestion
## `パスワード` のフロー
```

Original doc uses backquotes.

```suggestion
* フロントエンド (ユーザーのブラウザで実行中) は、`ユーザー名`と`パスワード`をAPIの特定のURL (`tokenUrl="token"`で宣言された) に送信します。
```

```suggestion
さらに、フロントエンドが**ユーザー名**と**パスワード**を使って、バックエンドで認証する方法を用意したいです。
```

```suggestion
**FastAPI**が提供するツールを使って、セキュリティを制御してみましょう。
```

```suggestion
まずはこのコードを使って、どう動くか観察します。その後で、何が起こっているのか理解しましょう。
```

日本語ではこのぐらいシンプルな方が自然な気がします。

```suggestion
    これは、**OAuth2**が `ユーザー名` や `パスワード` を送信するために、「フォームデータ」を使うからです。
```

`hoge` はそのままで、"fuga" は「fuga」に変換するように統一されてきています。
```suggestion 次のインタラクティブなドキュメントにアクセスしてください: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs。 ``` 好みの問題だと思うので微妙なら無視してください。
統一しました 🙇

review_comments: Why do we need a comma after "Path" when we already have "and".

This is a grammatical issue.
To be honest, I don't know how to use commas in this situation.

I don't think the comma in this case should be omitted in either American English or British English. Therefore, I add a comma.

- A, B and C
- **BETTER: A, B, and C**

How is it practically used?

Do not get me wrong your PR is perfectly fine by me, I was just curious as a non-native speaker.

I found [this](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Serial_comma). Seems like it is very common for native speakers to put a comma before the last item in a list. They are calling it the Oxford Comma.
Feel free to mark the conversation as resolved.

Thanks for your comments, @ycd.
I'm a non-native speaker, too.
I learned a lot from you.

title: 📝 Update/fix links in docs to use HTTPS
body:
The pattern of the first two commit. Replace files inside `docs` but exclude `*.svg` by

```
http://(?!127|local|0\.0|examp|192|flask|docs\.python|callbackhell|jinja|www\.celeryproject)
```

with

```
https://
```

The third commit:

```
http://flask.pocoo.org/ -> https://flask.palletsprojects.com
http://docs.python-requests.org -> https://requests.readthedocs.io
http://jinja.pocoo.org -> https://jinja.palletsprojects.com
http://www.celeryproject.org/ -> https://docs.celeryproject.org
```

```suggestion
Isso é útil para operações que precisam acontecer após uma solicitação, mas que o cliente realmente não precisa esperar a operação ser concluída para receber a resposta.
```

```suggestion
- Envio de notificações por email após a realização de uma ação:
```

```suggestion
O **FastAPI** criará o objeto do tipo `BackgroundTasks` para você e o passará como esse parâmetro.
```

```suggestion
Pode ser uma função `async def` ou `def` normal, o **FastAPI** saberá como lidar com isso corretamente.
```

```suggestion
- Quaisquer argumentos nomeados que devem ser passados ​​para a função de tarefa (`mensagem = "alguma notificação"`).
```

title: 🔧 Update GitHub Action Label Approved, run at 12:00
body:
🔧 Update GitHub Action Label Approved, run at 12:00.

It is currently having GitHub API rate limit errors, most probably because the Issue Manager that makes a lot of calls runs at the same time (at 00:00). So run this at 12:00.

title: ✨ Set response model defaults at application and router levels
body: This would allow for setting the default value of `response_model_by_alias`, `response_model_exclude_unset`, `response_model_exclude_defaults` and `response_model_exclude_none` at an application or at a router level, rather than on each individual endpoint

@frjonsen @astraldawn --
This would be useful for our team as we're having to set this on every API route.

What needs to happen to get this PR reviewed/considered for main?

Thanks for all your hard work 🙇 !

@cikay @frjonsen 
will be happy to see this PR merged, as my team as well need to set these flags in every API route
looks like this PR is also a bit outdated and has conflicts

will be happy to pick it up from here and open a new PR

> will be happy to pick it up from here and open a new PR

I have since moved on to different projects that don't use FastAPI, so feel free finish this yourself, if you want.
This pull request has a merge conflict that needs to be resolved.

Thanks for the interest and work on this! ☕

I want to implement a big refactor in how routers work that will solve many of these use cases.

I also need to do that before expanding the surface of what is supported with additional external PRs, because that also means I would have to keep maintaining and handling backwards compatibility for those new changes while doing that big refactor.

I have not wanted to close PRs that target the same or similar problems before I have an alternative, but then I have left many PRs open for too long, which is not useful or clear either. Sorry for that.

And then, after I make those big refactors, the problem might be solved... and if not, a bunch of conflicts will show up, so it will be better and easier to start from scratch (from the new code state).

So, I'll close this one for now to keep things clean.

For the specific use case, the best would be to document it in a new GitHub Discussion (or 👍 any existing one) with the specific problem to solve (without details of the implementation) to track the feature request.

Thank you! 🍰

review_comments:
```suggestion
デフォルトでは、**FastAPI** は [JSON互換エンコーダ](../tutorial/encoder.md){.internal-link target=_blank} で説明されている `jsonable_encoder` により、返す値を自動的にJSONに変換します。
```
PRにあるタイトルを使用。

```suggestion
これは多くの柔軟性を提供します。任意のデータ型を返したり、任意のデータ宣言やバリデーションをオーバーライドできます。
```
簡潔に

```suggestion
このようなケースでは、レスポンスにデータを含める前に `jsonable_encoder` を使ってデータを変換できます。
```
簡潔に

```suggestion
    また、`from starlette.responses import JSONResponse` も利用できます。
```
簡潔に

```suggestion
    **FastAPI** は開発者の利便性のために `fastapi.responses` という `starlette.responses` と同じものを提供しています。しかし、利用可能なレスポンスのほとんどはStarletteから直接提供されます。
```

```suggestion
例えば、Pydanticモデルを `JSONResponse` に含めるには、すべてのデータ型 (`datetime` や `UUID` など) をJSON互換の型に変換された `dict` に変換しなければなりません。
```

```suggestion
後のセクションで、カスタム `Response` を使用・宣言しながら、自動的なデータ変換やドキュメンテーションを行う方法を説明します。
```

この方が自然だと思います。

review_comments:
```suggestion
[レスポンスを直接返す](response-directly.md){.internal-link target=_blank}で見たように、 `Response` を直接返すことでこの挙動をオーバーライドできます。
```

```suggestion
使いたい `Response` クラス (サブクラス) をインポートし、 *path operationデコレータ* に宣言します。
```

```suggestion
[レスポンスを直接返す](response-directly.md){.internal-link target=_blank}で見たように、レスポンスを直接返すことで、*path operation* の中でレスポンスをオーバーライドできます。
```

```suggestion
`Response` を使って他の何かを返せますし、カスタムのサブクラスも作れることを覚えておいてください。
```
簡潔に

```suggestion
    **FastAPI** は開発者の利便性のために `fastapi.responses` として `starlette.responses` と同じものを提供しています。しかし、利用可能なレスポンスのほとんどはStarletteから直接提供されます。
```

```suggestion
    メディアタイプを指定せずにレスポンスクラスを利用すると、FastAPIは何もコンテンツがないことを期待します。そのため、生成されるOpenAPIドキュメントにレスポンスフォーマットが記載されません。
```

This is a long sentence, so separating the two sentences will make it easier to read.

```suggestion
    パラメータ `response_class` は、レスポンスの「メディアタイプ」を定義するために利用されます。
```

More simple. And the original sentence doesn't seem to emphasize that you can do that much.

```suggestion
    そして、OpenAPIにはそのようにドキュメント化されます。
```

This is more natural.

```suggestion
関数の中でレスポンスをオーバーライドしつつも、OpenAPI に「メディアタイプ」をドキュメント化したいなら、 `response_class` パラメータを使い、 `Response` オブジェクトを返します。
```

```suggestion
しかし、一方では `response_class` に `HTMLResponse` を渡しているため、 **FastAPI** はOpenAPIや対話的ドキュメントでHTMLとして `text/html` でドキュメント化する方法を知っています。
```

review_comments:
I believe we're using "rota" instead of "caminho", not sure tho.
```suggestion
Da mesma forma que voĉe pode declarar uma validação adicional e metadados nas *funções de operação de rota* com `Query`, `Path` e `Body`, você também pode declarar validações e metadados dentro dos modelos Pydantic usando `Field` do Pydantic.
```

```suggestion
    Observe que `Field` é importado diretamente de `pydantic`, não de `fastapi` como todo o resto (`Query`, `Path`, `Body`, etc.).
```
I used the ´caminho´ because in the other parts of the translation already using the 'caminho' instead of 'rotas'
If you search for `rotas` in the main page, you'll see it's there: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/pt/

review_comments:
Do we use underscore on foreign languages? If so:

```suggestion
Puedes declarar los "parámetros" o "variables" con la misma sintaxis que usan los *format strings* de Python:
```

Maybe the single quote notation is more suitable here? I'm not sure...
Talvez sea interesante poner en `Tips` que usamos la palabra "path": https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1218
*path*? `path`? O sin nada?
```suggestion Debido a que el schema generado es del estándar OpenAPI hay muchas herramientas compatibles. ```
Yeah, it makes sense, but at the same time, there's already a custom format for FastAPI-specific terms, like *path operation*, and for `variables` and `code`, so for now I'll leave it as it is.

"path" is explained here: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/es/tutorial/first-steps/#paso-3-crea-un-operacion-de-path so I think it's fine to not add more.

Also, adding a Tip here that is not in the original English version would start diverging from the original content, and it would be difficult to decide when to diverge and add more things and when not, so for now I'll leave it as is.
Sin nada por ahora.

Estoy viendo que abajo había varios con *path*, incluso en el original en inglés :sweat_smile: 

Me estaba faltando consistencia en eso, pero bueno, ya lo ajusté aquí.

title: multiple aliases on fields (fixes #1889)
body:
When completed, this PR will allow fields to be defined in this way as per https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1889:

```py
from typing import Optional
from pydantic import Required
from fastapi import FastAPI, Query

app = FastAPI()


@app.get("/")
def get(
        question: str = Query(Required, aliases=('q', 'question')),
        answer: Optional[int] = Query(None, aliases=('a', 'answer'))
    ):
    return {
        'question': question,
        'answer': answer,
    }


app.setup()
```

TODO:
- [x] Make backwards compatible with base pydantic models
- [x] Refactor the typing to make more sense. 
- [ ] Figure out how to communicate that a required value was not received for a field with multiple aliases.
- [ ] Update documentation.
- [ ] Add testing for MultiAliasableModelField class.
comments: 📝 Docs preview for commit ae465b87d6ab0f9da62046d602f63d34319e3400 at: https://5f933a06fc396a51dbd70e32--fastapi.netlify.app
I think this should first be handled by Pydantic side.

@Kludex Do you mean that Pydantic should natively support this? As I understood it Pydantic wasn't planning to support this but it seems like it would be a very useful feature for people trying to port their APIs.
I didn't know/forget about those information. Sorry 🥺

It's all good @Kludex! Thanks for the support @tomgrin10! Sorry for the lag, I've been pretty bust the last few days, but I'm hoping to make progress on this today.

Side note:
Does anyone have any idea how errors should be reported on aliasable fields?

title: ✨ Add instant docs deploy previews for PRs from forks
body:
✨ Add instant docs deploy previews for PRs from forks.

This uses the new GitHub Actions event [`workflow_run`](https://docs.github.com/en/free-pro-team@latest/actions/reference/events-that-trigger-workflows#workflow_run).

The GitHub Action event is currently not very well documented, and it ended up requiring a new custom action to extract the original PR for that docs deployment. But this should help with previews for changes in docs (e.g. translations). Instead of the current workaround of previews triggered every hour, this should have a preview in a comment ready in about 1 minute.

Hey @tiangolo, I was able to get this to work using pure workflows ([here](https://github.com/adriangb/scikeras/blob/master/.github/workflows/deploy_docs.yml) and [here](https://github.com/adriangb/scikeras/blob/master/.github/workflows/docs.yaml)) and some existing actions. Not that this is any better than what you've got, just sharing options 😄 

The main thing that helped in figuring out the `workflow_run` event (which as you say, isn't very well documented) is that you can do `echo ${{ toJSON(github.event.workflow_run) }}` to get all of the key/values in there.

Thanks @adriangb! Very interesting! 🤓 

> Sorry for the long delay! 🙈  I wanted to personally address each issue/PR and they piled up through time, but now I'm checking each one in order.

title: ♻️ Update black to 20.8b1 and apply new ruleset to codebase.
body: Just a quick update on black, and a more permissive update scheme (`>=` over `==`). Right now, having fastapi[tests] in our dev requirements nails our black to whichever version they have set. Having it like this allows us to update to newer versions quicker if we want to do so.

The word "Exparts" is misspelled.

Same as above.
Same as above.

"いくつかの言語のドキュメントはなかったでしょう。" is a little better because the sentences "Without them, there wouldn't be..." is probably subjunctive past.

https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/接続法#仮定法過去
The translation of the sentence "Here I'm also highlighting contributions from sponsors." is missing.

review_comments: This sentence lacks "に" after "アプリケーションの残りの部分".
It's better to modify "それ" to "レスポンス" because "それ" comes first, I don't know it refers to "レスポンス".
"CORS設定に追加する必要があります" may be a little better.
"requestと共に or 一緒に" may be a little better.

It seems unnatural that the same word appears twice in one short sentence.
But I have updated this line to make it more natural, so please check it again.
LGTM！

```suggestion
「ミドルウェア」は、すべての**リクエスト**に対して、それがあらゆる特定の*path operation*によって処理される前に機能する関数です。また、すべての**レスポンス**に対して、それを返す前に機能します。
```
Implication of "any specific"

```suggestion
* 次に、ミドルウェアはアプリケーション (の *path operation*) によって生成された**レスポンス**を受け取ります。
```
More natural representation.

```suggestion
* その後、`response` を返す前にさらに `response` を変更することもできます。
```
More natural representation.

@Kludex all good, my logic to reaching this was I ended up on Pydantic's site from FastAPI to read about how it's functioning, then I ended up on https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/mypy_plugin/ for their MyPy configuration, and set it to immediately find that FastAPI wasn't compatible with the strict mypy example configuration, which was unexpected.

I'm not sure tho.

I personally don't have a preference on this, but if it would help with running mypy with the default settings I think it would be a worthy change.

Thanks for the work and for the discussion here everyone! :coffee: 

This was implemented in https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2547, so I'm gonna close this PR now.

But thanks for the effort! :nerd_face:

This part is updated...😭

_지정_ 보다는 _선언_ 이 더 나아 보입니다. 😃

```suggestion
간단한 예제부터 시작해봅시다:
```

보여준다는 느낌이 강한 _예시_ 보다 _예제_ 가 더 나아보입니다.😊

Original: 
```
At some point you would have started the definition of the function, you had the parameters ready...
```

```suggestion
여러분은 매개변수를 준비했고, 함수를 정의하기 시작했을 겁니다.
```

```suggestion
이때 "첫 글자를 대문자로 바꾸는 함수"를 호출해야 합니다.
```

_당신_ 이라는 용어는 쓰지 않았으면 합니다. 어색하므로 아예 제하거나 _여러분_ 으로 바꾸었으면 좋겠습니다.

Please see how official python Korean docs translate "you"
https://docs.python.org/ko/3/search.html?q=당신
https://docs.python.org/ko/3/search.html?q=여러분

```suggestion
하지만 슬프게도 아무런 도움이 되지 않습니다:
```

```suggestion
### 타입 추가하기
```

```suggestion
저희는 이 함수의 매개변수 부분:
```

```suggestion
이게 다입니다.

이게 "타입 힌트"입니다:
```

```suggestion
등호(`=`) 대신 콜론(`:`)을 쓰고 있습니다.
```
```suggestion 만약 모든 자습서를 다 보았음에도 타입에 대해서 더 보고자 방문한 경우에는 `mypy`에서 제공하는 "cheat sheet"이 좋은 자료가 될 겁니다. ```

```suggestion
이 문서는 파이썬 타입 힌트에 대한 **빠른 자습서 / 내용환기** 수준의 문서입니다. 여기서는 **FastAPI**를 쓰기 위한 최소한의 내용만을 다룹니다. 
```

Hi, I suggest the updated translation for the updated part:
```suggestion
파이썬은 선택적으로 "타입 힌트(type hints)"를 지원합니다.

이러한 **타입 힌트**들은 변수의 <abbr title="예를 들면: str, int, float, bool">타입</abbr>을 선언할 수 있게 해주는 특수한 구문입니다.
```

```suggestion
타입힌트는 다음과 같이 기본 값을 선언하는 것과는 다릅니다:
```

It's a different thing.
```suggestion
이는 다른 것입니다.
```

How about this one? (just a tiny change of tone)
```suggestion
그렇지만 이제, 다시 함수를 만드는 도중이라고 생각해봅시다. 다만 이번엔 타입 힌트가 있습니다.
```

How about this one?
```suggestion
편집기가 변수의 타입을 알고 있기 때문에, 자동완성 뿐 아니라 에러도 확인할 수 있습니다:
```

title: WIP: Add API developer docs
body:
It's been months since issue #804 was created and since that date I've seen a lot of people interested on it. On there I also saw  @tiangolo 's wishes about someday using [mkautodoc](https://github.com/tomchristie/mkautodoc), but the framework is not maintained anymore. On the other hand, [mkdocstrings](https://github.com/pawamoy/mkdocstrings) is being under develop since that issue and now has achieved a much more mature state. Due to that fact, I'm opening this PR that has the following changes:

- Add `API Documentation` section on our documentation.
- Add `mkdocstrings` plugin on `mkdocs`.
- Add initial docstrings following [Google style](https://google.github.io/styleguide/pyguide.html).

Images:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7353520/97782779-c5f14d00-1b93-11eb-86a4-1bf5b8f90eb6.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7353520/97784005-c857a500-1b9b-11eb-9d30-e326ffb08c36.png)

Notes:
- Recommendations about how to document `Starlette` code are accepted.
- Copy/paste of Starlette code and FastAPI documentation itself will be used when possible.
- [Darglint](https://github.com/terrencepreilly/darglint) will be used with `-z full` or `-z long` flag. Accept suggestions.
- I haven't talked to @tiangolo about this, and I do not know his wishes about it. Please do not expect this to be merged, but if you like the idea, please add a :+1: here.

review_comments:
```suggestion
    De este modo, tendrás que asegurarte que los datos son del tipo correcto. Por ejemplo, que si corresponde con un JSON, si estás retornando un `JSONResponse`.
```

Hola @pedabraham porqué consideras necesario añadir *que* allí, a mí particularmente me parece que está de más para ello consideré [este  artículo](https://www.rae.es/dpd/que), por favor tener en cuenta que hay diferencias de sintaxis entre el español y el inglés y que los textos no deben quedar exactamente iguales; a menos que sea parte de los lineamientos para traducir la documentación, en ese caso haré el cambio pero si me puede hacer la aclaración estaré muy agradecida.

Hola @Xaraxx,

## Problema
El problema que yo vi fue sobre la condición siguiente: _si corresponde con un JSON, si estás retornando un `JSONResponse`_. 
No me fue muy claro que debía de asegurarme. Me hizo pensar inmediatamente en un condicional, y lo interprete de la siguiente forma:  siempre que se corresponde con un JSON, se está retornando un `JSONResponse`. Lo cual es falso y por eso debes de asegurarte de que eso pase.

## El **que**
El **que** fue mi forma de darle continuidad a la oración anterior para que se interpretara de la siguiente forma: De este modo, tendrás que asegurarte **que** si corresponde con un JSON, si estás retornando un `JSONResponse`.

## Propuesta
Ahora pues como tu no lo viste tan claro como yo (y ni siquiera yo la segunda vez), creo que debería cambiarse por algo diferente como lo siguiente:

```suggestion
    De este modo, tendrás que asegurarte que los datos son del tipo correcto. Por ejemplo, asegurarte que puedas tener un JSON, cuando estás retornando un `JSONResponse`.
```
Hola @pedabraham muchas gracias 👍  por aclararme ahora entiendo tu punto. Apenas tenga un tiempo hago el  cambio, estamos en contacto!

title: Enable the use of nested structures in peewee
body:
Hello, 

I love your lib, it is such a produtivity booster to me!

Another lib that I love is Peewee. One little problem that I have is the conversion from peewee to Pydantic models. When you have a nested model, the proposed getter on the documentation fails to convert it. With the proposed path, it convert the nested reference to model to json, and Pydantic take care of the right conversion.

📝 Docs preview for commit 02f9b2aa04cb566e9cb8e63b7b0257a4edd4757b at: https://5fa31764d2e3f833528a96d1--fastapi.netlify.app

Hey @bratao, thanks for your contribution!

I no longer work with Peewee, and I wouldn't recommend it because it doesn't play well with async/await as it depends on thread locals, and it also doesn't work well with type annotations.

I see the change you are proposing is simple, but I'm not sure how nested models work in Peewee, not sure if that saves them to JSON types, or if it makes automatic relationships with other tables. I fear if it's the second option it would easily end up in infinite recursion when serializing models.

Also, if I accept this I would need to understand it and then maintain it and answer questions about it, etc. And I would actually prefer to recommend other libraries that are made to be compatible with async and await, and that play better with type annotations.

Nevertheless, I think this could be a good candidate for an external blog post, with the tricks you have used to make it work properly for you. :sunglasses: If you write an article like that, you can add it to the external links section in the docs. :memo: 

Given that, I'll now close this PR, but thanks for your effort! :cake:

title: ⬆ Add support for Python 3.9
body:
Pydantic added support to Python 3.9 [last week](https://pydantic-docs.helpmanual.io/changelog/#v17-2020-10-26) on this [PR](https://github.com/samuelcolvin/pydantic/pull/1844). 

**TODO:**
- [X] Add tests with the new typing feature i.e. `dict[int]`

**Notes:** 
- I haven't talked to @tiangolo about this PR and I don't know his wishes towards this support. Please, do not assume this will be merged.
- Parametrize was not possible in the tests due to incompatibility of notation on Python versions and the fact that Pytest gets to discover the code even if the `skipif` mark is used.

```python
async def common_parameters(q: Optional[str] = None, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
```

title: ✨ Add Discord chat
body:
✨ Add Discord chat.

Also, hide a little the Gitter chat. It is currently too easy and almost "encouraged" to go and ask right away in the chat, instead of reading the docs and finding answers.

And a chat system allows too fluid conversations, without structure, so questions don't have clear examples, etc.

This means that the people that are helping others have to answer the same questions over and over again.

By hiding the chat a bit more, those who want to ask will have to at least try to read the docs, find previously answered questions, etc.

That way the chat can be more of a place to "hang out", share, etc. Instead of a support "hot line" that drains the few members that dedicate time to help others.

This also updates the section about "Help - Get Help", to encourage questions in GitHub issues, that way, help and answers from the community count for [FastAPI Experts](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/fastapi-people/#experts).

title: Add asynchronous sqlalchemy example
body:
Recently sqlalchemy released the beta for the 1.4 release, which includes support for asynchronous calls. 
This PR adds an example based on (https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/sql-databases/) but with asynchronous calls.

title: ⬆️  Upgrade Starlette to 0.14.2, including internal `UJSONResponse` migrated from Starlette
body:
starlette 0.14.1 includes a [fix](https://github.com/encode/starlette/pull/942) for users that want to use the `@requires` decorator from the `AuthenticationMiddleware` on FastAPI routes.

Breaking in starlette changes as far as I can see are:
 -  `UJSONResponse` was removed from the core [as per this issue](https://github.com/encode/starlette/issues/901), they added [documentation](https://www.starlette.io/responses/#custom-json-serialization) on how to implement custom JSON serialization. `UJSONResponse` is explicitly exported in FastAPI and should either be removed to follow starlette or added back in as done with `ORJSONResponse`. For this PR I chose to add a custom `UJSONResponse` and add test cases.

Is there any reason it was hard locked to 13.6 I am not aware of or is it as straightforward as updating the requirement?

title: Order routes parameters by name in OpenAPI
body:
Currently, routes' parameters are listed in the OpenAPI docs without a specific order, other than their type:
- path
- query
- headers
- cookie

In particular, to build the OpenAPI documentation for every route, the parameters are retrieved from each route (and related dependencies), and duplicated parameters are removed with dictionary comprehension.

**Problem**
In case there are multiple parameters, it may be a lot easier to show them sorted alphabetically in the documentation (with respect their type, so **path params** before **query params** for example). Also, the current order is not specified, since dictionary elements are in insertion order and it is not really clear how to control that. A better alternative (and easier to read in the doc) is to sort them by alphabetic order.

Example of parameters, taken directly from the current tutorial's tests:
```
"parameters": [
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Q", "type": "string"},
                        "name": "q",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Skip", "type": "integer", "default": 0},
                        "name": "skip",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Limit", "type": "integer", "default": 100},
                        "name": "limit",
                        "in": "query",
                    }
                ]
```

**Solution**
To fix this problem and have the parameters for each route nicely ordered by their name, it's sufficient to sort the parameters by the alias property when they are fetched from the routes.

The above piece of OpenAPI would become:
```
"parameters": [
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Limit", "type": "integer", "default": 100},
                        "name": "limit",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Q", "type": "string"},
                        "name": "q",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                    {
                        "required": False,
                        "schema": {"title": "Skip", "type": "integer", "default": 0},
                        "name": "skip",
                        "in": "query",
                    },
                ]
```

**Caveats**
I thought about two possible caveats:
1. There may be use-cases where someone would want parameters ordered with the current logic, which is by definition (e.g. parameter A is shown before parameter B if is defined before).
2. Some images in the repo would currently not be up-to-date (the order of parameters could be different), but I don't think it should be a big problem.

> maybe nice to have the option to use the original ordering? or to specify order?

I was thinking that alphabetical order in each parameter group is a better default - note that it's always possible to have the option to have any order [extending the OpenAPI documentation](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/extending-openapi/).

Thanks for the interest! I actually prefer to order parameters by the same definition order, to preserve the intention of the developer. If someone wants the parameters in a different order, they could order them in the code.

Sorting alphabetically doesn't necessarily bring the intention of the original order. For example, there might be some non-important parameters that start in `a`, those would end up being before other more important parameters.

So I'll pass on this one, but thanks for the interest! :coffee:

review_comments:
Awesome, having a `PydanticResponse` definitely sounds interesting and sweet.

I think you mean `response_model` here.
or `response_class=PydanticModel`, not sure but let's have both!
Thanks, I fixed it now :)

Why not name this class ModelResponse ?

Since it's response_model= ... and not response_pydantic= in the definition of the endpoint.
Fair point.

Also add a stringdoc to the class , cause it could be confusing that  "response_model" and this class exist in the framework for a newbie

```python
"""
This class let you respond with a pydantic model without the data validation ...
"""
```
Done!

why **"re"** validation ? Should it be **" by skipping validation and calls to `jsonable_encoder` by FastAPI."**
	
nothing protect from this case

```python
@app.get("/", response_model=Model)
async def get_pydantic() -> ModelResponse:
    a = Model(arg_str="toto", arg_int = 5)
    a.arg_int="tata"
    return ModelResponse(a)
```
Done.

title: 🐛 Fix edge case with repeated aliases names not shown in OpenAPI
body:
Currently, routes' parameters defined in multiple dependencies are not duplicated since only one parameter per position (path, query, headers, cookie) is retrieved. **The current solution has the caveat of not identifying parameters with the same alias, but different position.**

**Problem example**
Example of parameters shown in the current FastAPI Schema for the given route: 

```
@app.get("/{repeated_alias}")
def get_parameters_with_repeated_aliases(
    path: str = Path(..., alias="repeated_alias"),
    query: str = Query(..., alias="repeated_alias"),
):
    return {"path": path, "query": query}
```


```
'parameters': [{'in': 'query',
                                               'name': 'repeated_alias',
                                               'required': True,
                                               'schema': {'title': 'Repeated '
                                                                   'Alias',
                                                          'type': 'string'}}]
```

**Solution**
To fix the name clashing, it is sufficient to consider parameters duplicated only if they have the same alias and the same type.

The OpenAPI generated by the PR solution is:

```
"parameters": [
                    {
                        "in": "path",
                        "name": "repeated_alias",
                        "required": True,
                        "schema": {"title": "Repeated " "Alias", "type": "string"},
                    },
                    {
                        "in": "query",
                        "name": "repeated_alias",
                        "required": True,
                        "schema": {"title": "Repeated " "Alias", "type": "string"},
                    },
                ]
```

> maybe i'm a bit dense, but why do you need the same alias to a query and a path param?

Thank you, the path param and query is a corner case indeed and easily solvable by just changing the name of the query param (which is an implementation detail). On the other hand, consider a combination of query/cookie/header parameter. I agree it can be a corner case, but it is not an implementation detail and may be a particular requirement for an API to expose

And thanks for the input @falkben! :nerd_face:

In English it's the same. https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/path-params/#path-parameters
Ah... ok.

There's a markdown typo here.

```suggestion
Usando uma opção diretamente do Starlette, você pode declarar um *parâmetro de rota* contendo uma *rota* usando uma URL como:
```

This check is not showing correctly on the preview website. 

I believe the following suggestion will fix it.
```suggestion
!!! check
    Observe que o valor que sua função recebeu (e devolveu) é `3`, como o tipo Python `int`, não uma string `"3"`.

    Então, com essa declaração de tipo, **FastAPI** oferece a você uma <abbr title="convertendo a string que vem de uma requisição HTTP em dados Python">"conversão"</abbr> automática de requisição.
```

This is a PR to start the Persian documentation translation (lang=fa).

You can track Persian translations on this issue: #2041

Thanks a lot.

review_comments: I tried removing this config and it seems like `code` is still taken as `ltr`, would this still be needed?

Could you explain to me why it's better to have external-links as `ltr`? I just want to understand how it works.

Also, do you think the `display: inline-block` is needed? Why is that?

As I see that most of these settings only set some elements to always be `ltr`, I think it would be safe to move them to the main CSS (at `docs/en/docs/css/custom.css`). That file is then copied to all the languages.

Also because when the MkDocs configs are regenerated the custom CSS for Persian would be overridden, so, if these CSS styles can be put in a way that applies to everything, it will all work correctly. But if it's absolutely needed to have custom CSS styles only for right-to-left languages, then I would need to figure out a way to support it in the scripts that generate the multi-language docs. 😬

But that would also depend on if the `display: inline-block;` are necessary or not...

Hey Sebastián,

I added this because Persian language is RTL and I made the whole document RTL. But some parts of the doc including links and codes should always be LTR, no matter what the translation language is, otherwise they will be rendered in a bad way (Not readable actually). 

Also the `display: inline-block` is required because otherwise, some inline codes will not accept the `ltr` style.

So the answer is yes, please keep it as is.
Please read the above comment. It applies equally to `external-links`.

Yes, the changes that make some styles `ltr` are safe in every other language and it makes sense because they are applied to codes and links and these two items are always ltr, no matter what the language is. But I will pull the latest changes and check if everything is OK before resolving this thread.
I checked it. Yes it is totally safe to move these `ltr` changes to the main CSS.

review_comments:
括号也用全角，中英文之间加空格，全角标点前后不用加空格。 参考[排版规范](https://github.com/mzlogin/chinese-copywriting-guidelines/blob/Simplified/README.md)
```suggestion
**FastAPI** 不要求你使用 SQL（关系型）数据库。
```

adapt to 也有改编、改写的意思，在这里可能更恰当一点
```suggestion
您可以轻松地将其改写为 SQLAlchemy 支持的任何数据库，如：
```

```suggestion
* Microsoft SQL Server 等。
```

```suggestion
在本例中，我们将使用 **SQLite**，因为它使用单个文件，而且 Python 已经集成了支持。因此，您可以复制这个示例并按原样运行它。
```

虽然有些段落是分开的，但是翻译时最好还是结合上下文
```suggestion
之后，对于您的生产应用程序，您可能需要使用像 **PostgreSQL** 这样的数据库服务器。
```

根据 [中文出版物夹用英文的编辑标准](http://sxqx.alljournal.cn/uploadfile/sxqx/20190304/CY%20T154%E2%80%942017%20%E4%B8%AD%E6%96%87%E5%87%BA%E7%89%88%E7%89%A9%E5%A4%B9%E7%94%A8%E8%8B%B1%E6%96%87%E7%9A%84%E7%BC%96%E8%BE%91%E6%A0%87%E5%87%86.pdf) 似乎用英式引号也没问题吧。

review_comments:
```suggestion
**FastAPI**를 사용하면 매개변수에 대한 추가 정보 및 검증을 선언할 수 있습니다.
```

```suggestion
이 응용 프로그램을 예로 들어보겠습니다:
```

If you check [파이썬 한국어 번역](https://www.flowdas.com/pages/python-docs-ko.html) and search word, `application`, it is translated as `응용 프로그램` and because we follows the rules of that guide (check of it on #2017 ).

```suggestion
쿼리 매개변수 `q`는 `Optional[str]` 자료형입니다. 즉, `str` 자료형이지만 `None` 역시 될 수 있음을 뜻하고, 실제로 기본값은 `None`이기 때문에 FastAPI는 이 매개변수가 필수가 아니라는 것을 압니다.
```

> use less English as you can.

Check #2017 and there is no word `type` beside `None`.

```suggestion
매개변수와 일치해야 하는 <abbr title="정규표현식(regular expression), regex 또는 regexp는 문자열 조회 패턴을 정의하는 문자들의 순열입니다">정규표현식</abbr>을 정의할 수 있습니다:
```

```suggestion
**"정규표현식"** 개념에 대해 상실감을 느꼈다면 걱정하지 않아도 됩니다. 많은 사람에게 어려운 주제입니다. 아직은 정규표현식 없이도 많은 작업들을 할 수 있습니다.
```

Deprecated means "it will be not used in near future". So I left the original word, `deprecated`.
How about
```suggestion
이 매개변수를 사용하는 클라이언트가 있기 때문에 한동안은 남겨둬야 하지만, <abbr title="구식이며, 사용하지 않는 것을 추천">사용되지 않을 예정이다</abbr>라고 확실하게 문서에서 보여주고 싶습니다.
```
I updated it!

It's not a problem. Please see [국립국어원 Q&A - 괄호 뒤 조사](https://www.korean.go.kr/front/onlineQna/onlineQnaView.do?mn_id=216&qna_seq=115253).
I have the same opinion with 국립국어원.
So, I resolve this conversation.

review_comments: # 请求表单与文件
FastAPI 支持同时使用 `File` 和 `Form` 定义文件和表单字段。
接收上传文件或表单数据，要预先安装 `python-multipart`。
例如，`pip install python-multipart`。
## 导入 `File` 与 `Form`

title: ✨ Add support for shared/top-level parameters (dependencies, tags, etc)
body:
✨ Add support for shared/top-level parameters (dependencies, tags, etc) that can be set in `FastAPI`, `APIRouter`, and `include_router`.

Up to now, for several options, the only way to apply them to a group of *path operations* was in `include_router`. That works well, but the call to `app.include_router()` or `router.include_router()` is normally done in another file.

That means that, for example, to apply authentication to all the *path operations* in a router it would end up being done in a different file, instead of keeping related logic together.

Setting options in `include_router` still makes sense in some cases, for example, to override or increase configurations from a third party router included in an app. But in a router that is part of a bigger application, it would probably make more sense to add those settings when creating the `APIRouter`.

## In `FastAPI`

This allows setting the (mostly new) parameters (additionally to the already existing parameters):

* `default_response_class`: updated to handle defaults in `APIRouter` and `include_router`.
* `dependencies`: to include ✨ top-level dependencies ✨ that apply to the whole application. E.g. to add global authentication.
* `callbacks`: OpenAPI callbacks that apply to all the *path operations*.
* `deprecated`: to mark all the *path operations* as deprecated. 🤷
* `include_in_schema`: to allow excluding all the *path operations* from the OpenAPI schema.
* `responses`: OpenAPI responses that apply to all the *path operations*.

For example:

```Python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends


async def some_dependency():
    return


app = FastAPI(dependencies=[Depends(some_dependency)])
```

## In `APIRouter`

This allows setting the (mostly new) parameters (additionally to the already existing parameters):

* `default_response_class`: updated to handle defaults in `APIRouter` and `include_router`. For example, it's not needed to set it explicitly when [creating callbacks](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/openapi-callbacks/).
* `dependencies`: to include ✨ router-level dependencies ✨ that apply to all the *path operations* in a router. Up to now, this was only possible with `include_router`.
* `callbacks`: OpenAPI callbacks that apply to all the *path operations* in this router.
* `deprecated`: to mark all the *path operations* in a router as deprecated.
* `include_in_schema`: to allow excluding all the *path operations* in a router from the OpenAPI schema.
* `responses`: OpenAPI responses that apply to all the *path operations* in a router.
* `prefix`: to set the path prefix for a router. Up to now, this was only possible when calling `include_router`.
* `tags`: OpenAPI tags to apply to all the *path operations* in this router.

For example:

```Python
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends


async def some_dependency():
    return


router = APIRouter(prefix="/users", dependencies=[Depends(some_dependency)])
```

## In `include_router`

This allows setting the (mostly new) parameters (additionally to the already existing parameters):

* `default_response_class`: updated to handle defaults in `APIRouter` and `FastAPI`.
* `deprecated`: to mark all the *path operations* in a router as deprecated in OpenAPI.
* `include_in_schema`: to allow disabling all the *path operations* from showing in the OpenAPI schema.
* `callbacks`: OpenAPI callbacks that apply to all the *path operations* in this router.

Note: all the previous parameters are still there, so it's still possible to declare `dependencies` in `include_router`.

---

Related to: #1053, #1054 

This would supersede/include #1013

```suggestion
类似于 [增加 `依赖项` 到*路径操作装饰器*](dependencies-in-path-operation-decorators.md){.internal-link target=_blank}，您可以将他们添加到 `FastAPI` 应用程序中。
```

```suggestion
在这种情况下，它们将应用于应用程序中所有的*路径操作*：
```

```suggestion
所有关于 [增加 `依赖项` 到*路径操作装饰器*](dependencies-in-path-operation-decorators.md){.internal-link target=_blank} 一章中的点子仍然适用，但在本例中，适用于应用程序中的所有*路径操作*。
```

```suggestion
## 一组*路径操作*的依赖项
```

```suggestion
在之后，当您阅读如何构造可能包含多个文件的大型应用程序 ([大型应用程序-多个文件](../../tutorial/bigger-applications.md){.internal-link target=_blank})时，您将了解如何为一组*路径操作*声明单个 `依赖项` 参数。
```

@CyanBook It's not a typo: https://dictionary.cambridge.org/us/dictionary/english/clash
Anyway, "clash" isn't the proper verb to use. "Crash" is better imo.
📝 Docs preview for commit 4bf1282e7cb45463ff2c847978f40e60a0cb9b4f at: https://5fc7f7360837a800e17b83ce--fastapi.netlify.app

Thanks for your interest! But I actually think "clash" would be more appropriate here, as it wouldn't be possible for applications to take the same port twice, ad the same time, and overlap. One of the two applications will crash, so I think the word "clash" conveys that message better.

Given that, I'll pass on this one. But thanks for the effort! :coffee:
review_comments:
Based on your comment I've investigated and it looks like the most known term seems to be `overlap`. :thinking: 
```suggestion
as Uvicorn by default will use the port `8000`, the documentation on port `8008` won't overlap.
```
I agree with you. "overlap" is better than "crash"

review_comments:
```suggestion
이전 예제에서 보듯이, *경로 동작*은 아래와 같이 `Item` 속성을 가진 JSON 본문을 예상합니다:
```

`*path operation*`을 번역하면서 적절한 말이 없어 고민이 정말 많았고 지금도 그렇지만, 🥵
이 단어가 의마하는 것은 HTTP 메소드입니다.

["Operation" here refers to one of the HTTP "methods".](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/first-steps/#operation)

결국 그나마 빈번하게 사용하는 "이 API는 어떻게 '동작'한다"라는 구문처럼 '동작'으로 해석 해왔습니다.
저도 마음에 들지 않는 번역이나 여태까지 이렇게 작업해 왔기 때문에 동일하게 번역해주시면 좋겠습니다.

다만, 조금이라도 더 나은 대체어가 있다면 꼭! 제안해 주시고, 통일성을 위해 기존 번역들 역시 반영하겠습니다. 😁

정말 마음에 안 들고 적당한 번역이 없다면..원문 그대로 쓸까도 고려하고 있습니다.....

```suggestion
하지만, 다중 본문 매개변수 역시 선언할 수 있습니다. 예. `item`과 `user`:
```

가능하다면 직역해 주셨으면 합니다😉

```suggestion
복합 데이터의 검증을 수행하고 OpenAPI 스키마 및 자동 문서를 문서화합니다.
```

"유효성 검사"가 틀린 것은 아니지만, 이 역시 앞선 번역들에서 "검증"을 사용했습니다😅
오역인 것 같습니다😅

```suggestion
    `Body` 또한 `Query`, `Path` 그리고 이후에 볼 다른 것들처럼 동일한 추가 검증과 메타데이터 매개변수를 갖고 있습니다. 
```

Its original sentence is `Again, it will convert the data types, validate, document, etc.`.
There is no `**FastAPI**` here. In this case, you can make it more naturally by omitting the subject, "이것" or "FastAPI".

```suggestion
다시 말해, 데이터 타입, 검증, 문서 등을 변환합니다.
```

```suggestion
하지만, 만약 모델 내용에 `item `키를 가진 JSON으로 예측하길 원한다면, 추가적인 본문 매개변수를 선언한 것처럼 `Body`의 특별한 매개변수인 `embed`를 사용할 수 있습니다:
```

```suggestion
요청이 단 한개의 본문을 가지고 있더라도, *경로 동작 함수*로 다중 본문 매개변수를 추가할 수 있습니다.

하지만, **FastAPI**는 이를 처리하고, 함수에 올바른 데이터를 제공하며, *경로 동작*으로 올바른 스키마를 검증하고 문서화 합니다.
```

Don't break the style and format: Backtick (`) and asterisk(*) are rendered differently and have their own meanings. It's for protecting the original author's intentions.😎

```suggestion
기본적으로 단일 값은 쿼리 매개변수로 해석되므로, 명시적으로 `Query`를 추가할 필요가 없고, 아래처럼 할 수 있습니다:
```

title: Propagate callable's return type annotation for Depends/Security
body:
I was struck by the following paragraph in [tutorial for `Depends`](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/dependencies/classes-as-dependencies/#type-annotation-vs-depends):
https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/51e920e2fcf3e2a715081f28e3187549f2bf531a/docs/en/docs/tutorial/dependencies/classes-as-dependencies.md#L138-L150

I though that's easily solvable with [`typing.TypeVar`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.TypeVar), and this PR serves as a untested proof-of-concept, further corroborated only by the fact that the following short snippet passes mypy checks:
```py
from typing import Callable, TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')

class C:
    ...

def foo(x: Callable[..., T]) ->  T:
    return bar()

def bar():
    return ...

c: C = foo(C)   # <-- no error here
```
This alleviates the need for repeating the dependency (e.g. `CommonQueryParams`) in the annotation. :crossed_fingers:

review_comments:
I'm curious. Why not propagate the _T to the Depends? I mean, define a T (or another name there and import on this file as well) 

I'm asking this because of the ignore comment.

I'm no expert. `Depends` is a class:
https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/33be5fc8baa02bae949e1986f1821dae8f84f487/fastapi/params.py#L316
How would I type-override a class?

I think you can just make the class generic:
```py
from typing import TypeVar, Generic

T = TypeVar("T")


class Depends(Generic[T]):
    def __init__(
        self, dependency: Optional[Callable[..., T]] = None, *, use_cache: bool = True
    ):
        self.dependency = dependency
        self.use_cache = use_cache
    ...
```

```suggestion
首先，让我们来创建一个 Pydantic 用户模型。
```


“as a str“ 还像前面那样翻译可能更好一些

```suggestion
`get_current_user` 将使用我们创建的（伪）工具函数，该函数接收 `str` 类型的令牌并返回我们的 Pydantic `User` 模型：
```

原文档此处是加粗格式
```suggestion
现在你可以直接在*路径操作函数*中获取当前用户，并使用 `Depends` 在**依赖注入**级别处理安全性机制。
```

```suggestion
    在这里 **FastAPI** 不会搞混，因为你正在使用的是 `Depends`。
```
这里我特意将原文的顺序做了一些调整以符合中文的阅读习惯，比如我们我们通常会说「用户数据库模型」或「用户JSON模型」，而不会说「数据库用户模型」或「JSON用户模型」。
「和某某相同」应该有两个主体，但这里从原文看只有一个主体即`oauth2_scheme`，因此翻译成了同一个

```suggestion
现在让我们接着上一章继续开发，并添加缺少的部分以实现一个完整的安全性流程。
```

```suggestion
OAuth2 规定在使用「password 流程」（我们打算用的）时，客户端/用户必须将 `username` 和 `password` 字段作为表单数据发送。
```

这里不如所有的 scope 都不翻译（不管单复数），因为它本身是个表单字段名。如果个别地方翻译了，读起来会很怪，比如

> 这个表单字段的名称为 `scope`（单数形式），但实际上它是一个由空格分隔的「作用域」组成的长字符串。

```suggestion
    它有没有 `:` 这样的字符或者是不是 URL 都没有关系。
```

```suggestion
    这些细节是具体实现的。
```
用\<code>标签即”``“包括的不翻译，其余的翻译

📝 Docs preview for commit da96dbbaeccf6c68c188ad76b00c007fdb14338b at: https://5fd8a8a85a7b94dde96ccb8c--fastapi.netlify.app

@vjanz Based on what I've seen, I recommend you to:
- Create a "translation" issue e.g. https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/2041
- Divide this PR in two:
    - Language setup
    - `index.md` page
   
The reasons are:
- Being able to coordinate the translations, so others can contribute.
- To have translations merged, we need approvals from people that speaks the language. If there's a PR with only the setup, it can be merged right away and people can take their time to review the `index.md`. Besides, your PR will not block others to contribute with other pages.
@Kludex, i think he did it right :thinking:. Except creating a translation issue to coordinate translations :+1:
Did what? 🤔
I mean the setup, anything wrong with it?
Oh, I'm sorry. I assumed the index.md was already translated. 😔 Yeah, everything is fine!

Hey @Kludex and @ycd, thank you for starting the conversation here.

To be more clear, I just did the initial setup on this PR as requested on [docs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/contributing/), and I continued translating section by section on separate branches, so other people can contribute.

So I am thinking of making a separate PR for each section that I translate if that sounds right?

Cheers!

> To be more clear, I just did the initial setup on this PR as requested on docs, and I continued translating section by section on separate branches, so other people can contribute.

Yeah, it was my bad. I assumed the `index.md` was already translated. 

> So I am thinking of making a separate PR for each section that I translate if that sounds right?

It does! :tada:

Awesome! Thanks @vjanz ! :rocket: :tada: 

And thanks @Kludex and @ycd for the help here! :bow:

title: Fix adding prefix from APIRouter for websockets routes
body: Fix the behavior of prefixes for websockets, which was added for api routers in version [0.62.0](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/releases/tag/0.62.0)

title: Add newsletter directory for Chinese translation
body:
@tiangolo Since last commit #2509 , all existing translations need to add `overrides` directory manually, otherwise there will be an error when previewing the translation:
```
$ python ./scripts/docs.py live zh
ERROR   -  Config value: 'theme'. Error: The path set in theme.custom_dir ('/Users/waynerv/Repos/fastapi/docs/zh/overrides') does not exist.
Error: Aborted with 1 Configuration Errors!
```
comments:
Thanks for pointing it out! :mag:  I fixed it here, to not require translations for each language, at least to allow using previews for translations: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2541

---

Now the question is, should there be a notification translation in each language? I feel it could be misleading because people could subscribe expecting a newsletter in their language, and the newsletter would arrive in English.

Although the subscription form is in English, I'm not sure. What do you think @waynerv ?

> Thanks for pointing it out! 🔍 I fixed it here, to not require translations for each language, at least to allow using previews for translations: #2541
> 
> Now the question is, should there be a notification translation in each language? I feel it could be misleading because people could subscribe expecting a newsletter in their language, and the newsletter would arrive in English.
> 
> Although the subscription form is in English, I'm not sure. What do you think @waynerv ?

you may write some words like ‘the content of newsletter would be written in English' above the `SUBSCRIBE` if you decide to add it to all languages . By the way, people who studying and learning here should know some English more or less, so it won't be a problem.
@tiangolo I think @blt232018  has got a point. People choose to read documents in their native language just for convenience, but they may also be interested in newsletter in English.

Maybe it would make sense to translate the file `newsletter.md` 🤓 

For this one, as this is specifically for the override, as I would change this more frequently, for example when a new sponsor comes or leaves, taking the translation would mean that I would have to manually update each translation, and I don't speak the languages. 😅 

So I'll pass on this file. But I could take the one for `newsletter.md`. Thank you! 🤓 ☕

comments:
thx for review!

review_comments:
```suggestion
然后，浏览器会向后端发送一个 HTTP `OPTIONS` 请求，如果后端发送适当的 headers 来授权来自这个不同源（`http://localhost:8080`）的通信，浏览器将允许前端的 JavaScript 向后端发送请求。
```

```suggestion
* 特定的 HTTP 方法（`POST`，`PUT`）或者使用通配符 `"*"` 允许所有方法。
```

```suggestion
* 特定的 HTTP headers 或者使用通配符 `"*"` 允许所有 headers。
```

```suggestion
* 创建一个允许的源列表（由字符串组成）。
```

```suggestion
默认情况下，这个 `CORSMiddleware` 实现所使用的默认参数较为保守，所以你需要显式地启用特定的源、方法或者 headers，以便浏览器能够在跨域上下文中使用它们。
```

title: Update the Security Example to prevent Timing Attacks
body: Added a dummy verify password check to the examples to prevent timing attacks on the JWT example.
comments: This method [is deprecated](https://passlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/lib/passlib.context.html#passlib.context.CryptContext.dummy_verify). Is this still relevant and do you know an alternative?

@vrslev I looked at the example code provided by the passlib project and updated the code to reflect the new recommended way now that dummy_verify() is deprecated.

[Docs Link](https://passlib.readthedocs.io/en/stable/narr/context-tutorial.html?highlight=%E2%80%9Cnever%20verifying%E2%80%9D#verifying-migrating-existing-passwords)
📝 Docs preview for commit e7c76b5cea1ba3d8f031284894fa75dcbb0ee09d at: https://6272bb9fc439f4006f3a6d40--fastapi.netlify.app
📝 Docs preview for commit 247a255ef8996f11c3cae71642ae3142d5cbebe9 at: https://62850cb321d05834c0988d2e--fastapi.netlify.app
📝 Docs preview for commit 59abb022a65e001d6a79f8b7341be543d34cc0c2 at: https://62f5477099bc98008f65c7b4--fastapi.netlify.app

Thanks for the interest!

In this case, the information that the attacker would get is only if a user exists or not, but no information about the password.

And probably in most cases, the attacker could get that information more easily by trying to create a new account with that username, to detect when a username already exists.

So I would think this would depend more on the particular application, I don't think it's strictly necessary to add it here.

Also because adding it would require explaining what are timing attacks to the docs. And I'm already explaining it in another section. I wouldn't think it's necessary to add a long explanation about that for this section as there's no information about the password that can be exposed, only about the existence of users.

Given that, I'll close this one. But thanks for the effort! :coffee:

title: 📝 Update Uvicorn installation instructions to use uvicorn[standard] (includes uvloop)
body:
📝 Update Uvicorn installation instructions to use `uvicorn[standard]` (includes `uvloop`)

This was a recent change in Uvicorn, so plain `uvicorn` is now the minimal version, and `uvicorn[standard]` is the standard, high-performance, version.

Thank you for noticing @ArcLightSlavik seems like I fixed images from postman and slugs -- the whole time I thought it requires much more work than it actually was.

on third point I'll change that according to recommendation, but wondering why? Making separate in and out models and also separate ORM Model seems like a tons of code repeating almost the same thing every time. Is there any way to avoid this?

Your confusing orm model and pydantic model. Orm model should include every single field in a table, but you wouldn't want to return every piece of information in the response all the time, this is where pydantic comes in. 
Say you have 100 fields in your table but you only want to return 2, your `response_model_exclude` would have to include every field in there and then it would be repeated for any other endpoint you might make(!). By making multiple pydantic models you don't have to duplicate it. For me response_model_exclude is more of `only **this** endpoint doesn't need this field`.

In tortoise it would be something like this? (pseudo code)
```
SoftwareEngineer_Pydantic = pydantic_model_creator(
    SoftwareEngineers, name="SoftwareEngineer"
)
SoftwareEngineer_Out_Pydantic = pydantic_model_creator(
    SoftwareEngineers, name="SoftwareEngineerOut", exclude=('password')
)
```
<https://github.com/tortoise/tortoise-orm/blob/develop/tortoise/contrib/pydantic/creator.py#L117>

Thanks for the help and suggestions @ArcLightSlavik !

Thanks for the article @trkohler ! :nerd_face: 

Nevertheless, when I try to access it I'm getting a 404. Maybe you updated the URL?

yes, I have and forgot about it :(
Let me update the url in PR!

[While I'm in process, this is a new url which should work](https://trkohler.com/fast-api-introduction-to-framework)
I fixed the link in PR, should work correctly now

title: Prefer custom encoder over defaults if specified.
body:
In encoders.py/jsonable_encoder, default encoders for each type are specified first & are used in preference to custom encoders.

This affects custom encoders specified for certain types like enums such that the default encoder is always executed and any custom encoders specified are ignored. See - #1986 - for a description of this issue and example code as well that illustrates the problem.

This PR adds a change to prefer custom encoders if specified in preference to default encoders.

title: ✨ Enable configuring Swagger UI parameters
body: At present there are a number of [Swagger UI parameters](https://swagger.io/docs/open-source-tools/swagger-ui/usage/configuration/) that are not configurable when using FastAPI. Under normal circumstances most of these wouldn't need to be used at all, however there are some cases where you might need to override some of these. For example, Swagger UI's syntax highlighting is [known to be slow](https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/3832), and being able to easily disable this would be very useful in certain situations.
comments: 📝 Docs preview for commit b6d0d6424865fa8fbd49d4ec12e2012dab42dc27 at: https://5fe42722500f90b60ec3643e--fastapi.netlify.app

review_comments: typo: `must be a dict`
Good catch, thanks!
I think the second parameter of this line `Dict[str, str]` can be also an integer so I suggest to change it into  `Dict[str, any] `
Good point, changed.
Nice!

To follow the current JS rendering, you should add an `\n` at the end.
I don't think it's a good idea to pass JS code in argument `{value}`. It's easy to break all the javascript rendering in the browser. Why not using JSON to transform python valid values into JS?
```
>>> json.dumps(False)
'false'
>>> json.dumps("string")
'"string"'
>>> json.dumps(1)
'1'
```
Great point, I've made the changes and rebased onto master.

title: Update starlette to `~=0.14`
body:
Just seeing if there is any appetite to:
  1. Update the `starlette` pin
  2. Loosen the exact pinning
comments: 📝 Docs preview for commit 0b4b0e79cc966fa849ba55e77093dea9a53b0e54 at: https://5fe427eb69f90dbff37cd5dd--fastapi.netlify.app
This is a duplicate of https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2335

Looks like it's not compatible due to the removal to `UJSONResponse`:
```
fastapi/responses.py:10: error: Module 'starlette.responses' has no attribute 'UJSONResponse'; maybe "JSONResponse" or "Response"?
```
***xref:*** https://github.com/encode/starlette/releases/tag/0.14.1

> This is a duplicate

So, it is - sorry should have searched first!

I still think there's value in relaxing the exact pinning so wanted to check if @tiangolo had changed his position since #1555?

Thanks for the discussion! 🍰 

And nope, I can't relax the pin yet, there have been constant/recent breaking changes. And in fact, the current pin on Starlette allows Starlette to move more easily without worrying a lot about breaking stuff between versions and having to deal with a lot of backwards compatibility between versions. That problem will probably come later after the first 1.x versions, but for now, it's better to try to structure the code the best way possible before getting into those new problems... 🤓 

> Sorry for the long delay! 🙈  I wanted to personally address each issue/PR and they piled up through time, but now I'm checking each one in order.

title: ✨ Add support for `option` argument in ORJsonResponse
body:
✨ Add support for the `option` argument of orjson when building an ORJsonResponse. 

The `orjson.dumps()` function supports an `option` argument [1] where flags can be specified. 
This PR makes it available when creating am `ORJSONResponse`.

For example, to support serializing numpy arrays
```python
return ORJSONResponse(content, option=orjson.OPT_SERIALIZE_NUMPY)
```

[1][ https://github.com/ijl/orjson#option]( https://github.com/ijl/orjson#option)

review_comments:
```suggestion
헤더 매개변수를 `Query`, `Path` 그리고 `Cookie` 매개변수들과 같은 방식으로 정의할 수 있습니다.
```

```suggestion
먼저 `Header`를 임포트합니다:
```

```suggestion
`Path`, `Query` 그리고 `Cookie`를 사용한 동일한 구조를 이용하여 헤더 매개변수를 선언합니다.
```

Original was:

```
It is possible to receive duplicate headers. That means, the same header with multiple values.
```

```suggestion
중복 헤더들을 수신 할 수 있습니다. 즉, 다중값을 갖는 동일한 헤더를 뜻합니다.
```

It's really nice and natural🤩, but we should not break a sentence structure as much as possible. I also mentioned it in another review because we have to preserve the original author's intentions.
Thank you. You're right. I think I made a mistake.
