review_comments: И если вы это сделаете, ваше окончательное предложение будет иметь такую же потерю производительности, как и при использовании фреймворка, который позволяет вам уменьшить количесво кода вашего приложения и багов соответственно.
С маршрутизацией по путям
Так же как Starlette берёт за основу/использует
то есть он не может быть быстрее Starlette
(Там пришлось бы реализовывать) => Таким образом, ваше итоговое приложение имело бы такие же накладные расходы ...
34 строка

```suggestion Независимые бенчмарки TechEmpower показывают, что приложения на **FastAPI**, запущенные под Uvicorn, , являются одними из самых быстрых среди доступных Python-фреймворков, медленнее только самих Starlette и Uvicorn (используемых внутри FastAPI). (*) ```

```suggestion
В частности, Uvicorn, Starlette и FastAPI сравниваются вместе (среди многих других инструментов).
```

```suggestion
Чем проще проблема, которую решает инструмент, тем выше его производительность. И большинство бенчмарков не тестируют дополнительные функции, предоставляемые инструментом.
```

```suggestion
    * Вы не будете писать приложение на Uvicorn напрямую. Это означало бы, что ваш код должен включать как минимум весь 
```

```suggestion
    код, предоставляемый Starlette (или **FastAPI**). И если вы так сделаете, то в конечном итоге ваше приложение будет иметь те же накладные расходы, что и при использовании фреймворка, который минимизирует код вашего приложения и ошибки.
```

```suggestion
    * Так же, как Starlette использует Uvicorn и не может быть быстрее его,  **FastAPI** использует Starlette, и поэтому не может быть его быстрее.
```

```suggestion
    * FastAPI предоставляет больше возможностей поверх Starlette. Возможности, которые наверняка вам понадобятся при создании API, такие как проверка данных и сериализация. Используя FastAPI, вы получаете автоматическую документацию бесплатно (автоматическая документация даже не увеличивает накладные расходы на запущенные приложения, она генерируется при запуске).
```

```suggestion
    * Если вы не использовали FastAPI, а использовали Starlette напрямую (или другой инструмент вроде Sanic, Flask, Responder и т.д.), то вам приходилось самостоятельно реализовать всю валидацию и сериализацию данных. Таким образом, ваше конечное приложение имело бы такие же накладные расходы, как если бы оно было создано с помощью FastAPI. И во многих случаях большая часть кода, написанного в приложениях, отвечает за валидацию и сериализацию данных.
```
@solomein-sv В варианте с "нужен", я считаю, есть неоднозначность и кто-то может не понять, "кто кому" нужен... Если не обладает нужными знаниями, конечно.
@TemaSpb `него -> его` в данном случае точно ошибочно. Насчет запятой не уверен, возможно, @solomein-sv еще посмотрит, но я бы оставил все так, как есть, кроме запятой (в ней не уверен).

@TemaSpb я б взял `Используя FastAPI`, `его` тут действительно не очень клеится, в остальном же, я думаю, можно оба варианта использовать. 

@solomein-sv, что думаешь?

title: ⬆️ Upgrade OpenAPI from 3.0.2 to 3.1.0
body:
Following [this document](https://www.openapis.org/blog/2021/02/16/migrating-from-openapi-3-0-to-3-1-0).

## Changes
- Bump version (and check if the links are valid on the docs)
- Support `examples`

## Observations
Nothing to do on `exclusiveMinimum` and `exclusiveMaximum`, as we were already compliant based on the JSON Schema version that Pydantic uses.

Update: I got blocked by https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/5891. The latest swagger doesn't support OpenAPI 3.1.0 yet. 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7353520/113490253-2c72f200-94c9-11eb-8960-462e75ab0f96.png)

@Kludex, this is terrific! 

In general, migration to OpenAPI 3.1.0 should give us a lot of improvements (like full compatibility with JSON Schema) and fixing the problem with `exclusiveMinimum` and `exclusiveMaximum` (https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/240 - because of it, currently generated OpenAPI files are invalid and cause errors).

However, it seems that SwaggerUI will not support OpenAPI 3.1.0 anytime soon (at least not clear from https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/5891). Do you consider supporting OpenAPI 3.1.0 regardless SwaggerUI, and simply relying on the fact that Redoc already supports 3.1.0?

There are already a lot of tools supporting 3.1.0 - https://openapi.tools/ - and in my opinion the slow speed of SwaggerUI development shouldn't affect that much the development speed of FastAPI.

Alternatvely, of course, we can focus on fixing at least https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/240 to make OpenAPI files valid for 3.0.3 and usable with the ecosystem of other tools (like Postman, Stoplight, Spectral, etc.).

> Do you consider supporting OpenAPI 3.1.0 regardless SwaggerUI, and simply relying on the fact that Redoc already supports 3.1.0?

Well... Yeah, generating a valid schema is something I'm interested in, as there are tools/services like https://readme.com/ that need a valid schema. Also, having it for ReDoc at least, and wait for https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/5891 on Swagger's side looks fine.

In any case, I'll not perform further work without @tiangolo's input.

EDIT (11/05/2022): I think I'm actually coming back to it soon.

@Kludex @a-akimov Following the [latest update](https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/5891#issuecomment-1172274595) from SmartBear, it looks like it will take many more months before we see OpenAPI 3.1 support in Swagger UI.
In the meantime, maybe it's worth having an opt-in option for developers to allow FastAPI to generate OpenAPI 3.1 (even though it will break Swagger UI)? 🤷🏻‍♂️
Hey everyone, any updates on this?
No.

I think FastAPI should probably push ahead with supporting OpenAPI 3.1. The spec was finalized 2 years ago at this point, so it seems unclear when if ever Swagger UI will support OAI 3.1.

Can we not do both? Allow people to choose if they want a 3.0 or 3.1 spec generated (or both!) with the knowledge that opting for 3.1 means no Swagger UI (but ReDoc has compat so I don't think that should be a dealbreaker).

Stopping progress in the name of a dep that doesn't seem interested in progress hardly seems like the right move.
FastAPI will only support OpenAPI 3.1 after pydantic v2.

review_comments: I think it sounds better in plural as in the original english text

- Use single term "аннотации типов" through all the text to avoid inconsistency.
- Sounds better: "у них много выгод и преимуществ" instead of "они дают ему много преимуществ и выгод"
"об аннотациях типов"
In case of "function" the closer translation is "Принимает", not "Берет"
"Преобразует"
@BugLight yes, it makes sense, thanks
@BugLight I thought if it sounds better and I'm not sure. What is the motivation?

@BugLight Где там "пользовательский" в оригинале? И по смыслу тоже не подходит. Возможно, я б заменил "как" на "в качестве", но это больше вкусовщина, я думаю. Как и замена "объявить" на "использовать"...

There are two kinds of verbs in russian grammar: совершенного вида and невосовершенного вида. "Преобразует" describes completed action, "преобразовывает" describes the process. It's a little more complex than my explanation, but this is the key difference. At most I rely on my native speaker's intuition.

PS Можно по литературе соответствующей посмотреть, нигде не встречал "преобразовывает"
Не посмотрел в оригинал. Вне контекста о другом подумал.
I know the difference between those variants and I translated it exactly because of the process accent, honestly.
ок, без проблем 👍
https://context.reverso.net/перевод/английский-русский/converts#преобразует
аргумент, тогда обновляю 👍

title: 🌐 Add German translation for `docs/de/docs/index.md`, `docs/de/docs/fastapi-people.md`, and `docs/de/docs/python-types.md`
body:
Hello,

I have started to translate the documentation into german (to be honest, I was a bit surprised that nothing existed here yet).
Currently I have translated the following chapters.
- index
- features
- fastapi-people
- python-types

I thought to translate them piece by piece just following the order of the mkdocs nav.
Since I have never worked with MkDocs, I hope the "setup" for the german section is correct.

_P.S.: Please don't be surprised about the short time intervals of the commits. No, I am not so fast in translating - It took me a while. I only had a bad setup near the end and had to reset everything. ._

This is awesome @0x4Dark! :rocket: You have done a lot of work! :bow: :clap: I have to wait for a couple of reviews before merging it. And as it has translations for several files, it will probably be difficult for someone to translate them all at the same time. --- Let me suggest, could you split it into several PRs?
Edit 2023-06-26: this part of the comment is no longer necessary, just one PR per file, that's it! 🎉 One would be just the basic setup, without any translation, just the result of: ```console $ python ./scripts/docs.py new-lang de ```
~And then~ a new PR for each file translated. That way, even if there are some changes for some of them, maybe one of them is already perfect and can be approved and merged faster.

Hi all,
Unfortunately, had the last few months no time to continue working on it and then forgot. Too many projects (of course with FastAPI).

Thanks for the feedback @AaronDewes.

@tiangolo I can do that, no problem. I'll work the changes from AaronDewes and create the pull requests asap.

I updated it with recent changes, there's no longer need to use and synchronize complex `mkdocs.yml` files. I also removed the files that were already translated in other PRs and resolved the conflicts.

Nevertheless, it would probably be easier and better to have these 3 files in 3 different PRs so that it's easier to review them, and once one of the files is fine and approved (even if the other two are not), I could merge that if it was in an isolated PR.

As I know you're busy, maybe others might want to copy some of the work done here in one of these files and make a new PR only for that one, and continue from there. 🤓

title: 🔧 Replace Flit by Poetry
body:
Given your message at https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/1555#issuecomment-643678310:

> On the other hand, I'm thinking of migrating from Flit to Poetry, that way I could set up Dependabot to make it send PRs for each new version.

You said "thinking", but I went ahead, and prepared this PR in order to enable you to add [dependabot](https://dependabot.com/) or [renovate](https://docs.renovatebot.com/).

I didn't get your confirmation, so I'll understand if this is closed. :kissing: :+1: 

P.S.: This PR has 2.2k lines, but 2.1k are from `poetry.lock` :sweat_smile: 

## Plus

In case you want to enable [renovate](), you'll need to go to [GitHub Apps](https://github.com/apps/renovate) and configure the bot for FastAPI. Besides, I've also prepared a basic [renovate](https://docs.renovatebot.com/) config compatible with gitemoji: https://github.com/Kludex/fastapi/pull/1 . But in case you want to check more configuration possibilities, just click [here](https://docs.renovatebot.com/configuration-options/).

Oh, and that PR already shows you the real scenario on what PRs will be opened in case you configure this bot. But as a preview:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7353520/114254555-6011b880-99b0-11eb-9ce3-eef39b556d4f.png)

I think this should be
```suggestion
requests = { version = ">=2.24.0,<3.0.0", optional = true }
```

And the same change for all optional dependencies in `[tool.poetry.dependencies]` and `[tool.poetry.dev-dependencies]`
I recommend pinning Poetry version here to get truly reproducible builds, and avoid issues if/when lockfile format changes or similar issues.
To clarify: in the current state this line would mean requiring `requests[all]` but we dont want that. We want to install requests given `fastapi[all]`

title: 🔒 Match HTTP Digest specs from RFC 7616
body: Closes #1476 ## Changes :sparkles: : - Add `realm` as specified [1](#response-headers). - Add optional `algorithm` header. If not present, should use `MD5`, as specified [1](#response-headers). ## TODO: - [ ] Implement the changes based on RFC 7616. - [ ] Documentation. ## Decisions - I didn't add the `domain` parameter on the `HTTPDigest` class, as it's optional and I've noticed that some implementations ignores its existence. ### References - https://docs.python.org/3/library/hashlib.html - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digest_access_authentication - https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2617 - https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/Flask-HTTPAuth/blob/master/flask_httpauth.py - https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9534602/what-is-the-difference-between-digest-and-basic-authentication ### Footnotes: 1: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7616#section-3.3 #### Updates - 2021-04-11: Add support to `algorithm` and `realm`.

title: fix jsonable_encoder exclude include encode model obj invalid
body:
Hi, When i use the `jsonable_encoder` expect covert model to dict obj，use the `exclude` or `include` options  was invalid

```
data = jsonable_encoder(queryset, exclude={'id'})
```

or

```
data = jsonable_encoder(queryset, include={'name'})
```

The `id` always in the data

So i created this  pr to solve this problem, Maybe there are better ways to solve this problem

> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3078?src=pr&el=footer&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez). Last update [2252837...f0adeb7](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3078?src=pr&el=lastupdated&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez).

For someone reviewing this PR: this was raised in #2594, and the open PR for the fix is #2606. There is more than just including the "include" and "exclude".

As of this fix @shan-guo, did you considered nested objects? With your proposed solution, the include and exclude will be recursively passed to the children and they will also be included and excluded, and I am not sure if that is the expected behaviour. For this reason in #2606, I just included "include" and "exclude" to the last "jsonable_encoder". Pydantic solves this recursivity problem by allowing a nested dictionary for "include" and "exclude", but this is not solved either in this PR and in the #2606. It is a different problem to solve.

For the first "include" and "exclude" you passed (BaseModel) is not needed since it is passed to the BaseModel.dict function just above, so it is redundant.

For the second one (dict), it is not needed for what I mentioned before, it will include and exclude children, which I don't think it is exptected.

For the third one (default) is needed since it will be needed for the dict case. This is why this was included in #2606.

Furthermore, the #2606 fixes the problem with include and exclude for dict objects to work like they work in Pydantic BaseModel.

You can see the added tests in #2606 for the use cases.

Regards,
Xavi M.

title: Allow parameterized router subclasses in applications.
body:
This allows developers to to provide custom router subclasses to their FastAPI applications for further customization.

Documentation for this feature added [here](https://6075131e18fa27238205260c--fastapi.netlify.app/advanced/custom-request-and-route/#custom-apirouter-class-in-an-application).

Closes #3079.

Is this substantially different than just subclassing `APIRoute` as detailed here? https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/custom-request-and-route/#custom-apiroute-class-in-a-router

EDIT: sorry, I came here directly from the issue. I now see this adds the ability to subclass APIRouter in addition to Request and APIRoute.

In my case, I've created my own subclasses APIRouter in order to autmatically attach a custom default Request class. It's not clear to me that adding the hook in FastAPI is really needed here.

For your specific example of adding a HEAD endpoint for every GET endpoint, if the endpoint would set custom response headers, it seems like this might miss those.

> In my case, I've created my own subclasses APIRouter in order to automatically attach a custom default Request class. It's not clear to me that adding the hook in FastAPI is really needed here.

I think subclassing `APIRouter` is the Golden Path here, this mostly addresses an API inconvenience to make it easier to set your subclass as the toplevel router. I definitely see the argument for it not being widely applicable enough to merit complicating the framework configuration `kwargs`, though.

> For your specific example of adding a HEAD endpoint for every GET endpoint, if the endpoint would set custom response headers, it seems like this might miss those.

One of the main things you get with custom router subclasses (as opposed to custom route subclasses) is the opportunity to wrap the endpoint differently for different routes—my method example here is probably not very demonstrative, but it's the simplest one I could come up with for documentation/unit testing purposes.

A better example of what you can do with a custom router might be then having the router wrap the `GET` endpoint's return value to return an empty body per  the`HEAD` spec, but it appears something in the stack is smart enough to do that for me. I was expecting to have to nullify the response myself to get [this test](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3080/files#diff-50104d2aea1851fe66e4fcd1954a5e68dc59c9279a46c6c6bb5b957c34a89203R69-R72) to pass, but there was no need.

---

Feel free to close if you don't think this brings enough value. The dive into the source code was illuminating for me (excellent codebase, FWIW) and I'm comfortable navigating this in my own apps, so I've got what I need to succeed!

title: Allow OpenAPI extensions
body:
The fastapi documentation describes how you can provide additional open api config: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/schema-extra-example/#pydantic-schema_extra

However, it does not work openapi extensions: https://swagger.io/docs/specification/openapi-extensions/

review_comments: ++ El parámetro de consulta `q` es de tipo `Optional[str]`. Eso significa que es de tipo `str` pero tambien podria ser `None`, y de hecho, el valor predeterminado es `None`. Así FastAPI sabrá que no es necesario.
++ El `Optional` en `Optional[str]` no es usado por FastAPI, pero le permitirá a tu editor de texto darte un mejor soporte y detectar errores.
++ Vamos a enfatizar que aunque `q` es opcional, siempre que se proporcione, **su longitud no supere los 50 caracteres**.
++ Para lograr esto, primero debes importar `Query` desde `fastapi`:
++ Usar `Query` como el valor predeterminado

Este fue el cambio que realicé en esta recomendación: 
```suggestion
Si te sientes perdido con todo estos de **"expresiones regulares"**, no te preocupes. Son un tema difícil para mucha gente. Todavía puedes hacer muchas cosas sin necesitar expresiones regulares todavía.
```
dejé `estos` en lugar de `eso` porque  en ingles estan usando `these`, que seria el plural de `this`. :)

me parece mejor mantener `con` porque se esta haciendo uso de la instancia `Query` y no convirtiendose en ella.

unas lineas mas abajo:
q: Optional[str] = Query(None, min_length=3)
me parece que la traducción está bien.

```suggestion
Cuando no necesitemos declarar más validaciones o metadatos, podemos hacer el parámetro de consulta `q` sea requerido simplemente dejando de declarar un valor predeterminado, como:
```

> `Δ = absolute <relative> (impact)`, `ø = not affected`, `? = missing data`
> Powered by [Codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3127?src=pr&el=footer&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez). Last update [2252837...9f9d5f5](https://codecov.io/gh/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/3127?src=pr&el=lastupdated&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez). Read the [comment docs](https://docs.codecov.io/docs/pull-request-comments?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=Sebasti%C3%A1n+Ram%C3%ADrez).
📝 Docs preview for commit 9f9d5f5f438d3c8cba24eef489f466ee6ec4f2f7 at: https://60841134cf0f8749477ad4ac--fastapi.netlify.app
@tiangolo Would you mind reviewing this PR?

Thanks for the interest and effort! But I prefer to leave it as is, because dataclasses require assuming some knowledge about dataclasses and how they work. And they would only work for some specific cases. They wouldn't necessarily take things like `Query()`, `Body()` as default values, or at least the behavior is not certain. And it would require explaining how they work.

I prefer to keep the explanation simple and pure, that makes it obvious that the thing that makes it callable is what would be used to get the information (in this case the `__init__()`. But in a dataclass that is hidden, so it would require explaining that it's hidden but created automatically, and then it would require explaining that because the `__init__()` is autocreated, some things would work and some wouldn't.

And although the code is shorter, the explanation and the cognitive load would be higher, explaining what's everything that is happening underneath.

So I'll close this one, but thanks for the effort! :cake:

title: 📝 Update docs with pip install calls when using extras with brackets, use quotes for compatibility with Zsh
body: Square brackets in zsh have special meanings. Copy and run `pip install uvicorn[standard]` in zsh will get error.
comments: It will not.

I had this problem before and now I finally know how to solve it.zsh uses [square brackets for globbing / pattern matching](http://zsh.sourceforge.net/Guide/zshguide05.html#l137).

I've never seen it (add quotation mark) in any other documentation, but add a notice about this problem should be nice.
@waynerv [python-language-server](https://github.com/palantir/python-language-server)  add quotation mark on install command `pip install 'python-language-server[yapf]'` in its documentation

Just adding the quotes as @tomwei7 suggested seems fine.

Why I don't have this problem here tho? :sweat_smile:
run `zsh -f` start zsh without any config that can reproduce the problem. `setopt nonomatch` in zsh will avoid this problem.
📝 Docs preview for commit 69bd84dd7fc7f5ba091f99505ee1cf0aa02d9c87 at: https://60a23ab30a1ff163fafcd679--fastapi.netlify.app

Thanks @tomwei7! :cake: 

I updated it to include all the other calls of `pip install` that were using packages with extras.

title: ♻ Change a `dict()` for `{}` in `fastapi/utils.py`
body:
In general `dict()` call is considered slower than direct `{}`.
The internal representations are also different, there is no extra function call while constructing the map with `{}`
```
>>> import dis
>>> dis.dis('{}')
  1           0 BUILD_MAP                0
              2 RETURN_VALUE
>>> dis.dis('dict()')
  1           0 LOAD_NAME                0 (dict)
              2 CALL_FUNCTION            0
              4 RETURN_VALUE
```

There are bunch of similar cases in FastAPI source code, I will send PRs accordingly.

title: ✨ Allow hiding from OpenAPI (and Swagger UI) `Query`, `Cookie`, `Header`, and `Path` parameters
body:
Add parameter `include_in_schema` to partially address #1708 for subclasses of `Param`: `Cookie`, `Header`, `Path` and `Query`

Implements the `Param` portion of https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1708#issuecomment-663366079, relevant parts of code sample reproduced below:

> ```python
> from fastapi import FastAPI, Query
> 
> app = FastAPI()
> 
> 
> @app.get("/")
> def read_root(hidden_param: str = Query(None, include_in_schema=False)):
>     if hidden_param:
>         return {"Hello": "Master!"}
>     else:
>         return {"Hello": "World"}
> ```

review_comments:
Maybe 

```python
if not field_info.include_in_schema:
    continue
```

This will decrease 1 indent level to the rest of codes in loop.
Also people can skip finding the `else` block of this if while reading these codes

[discussion] don't think should/could hide path parameter, the path parameter already shown during route declaration. For example:


```python
@app.get('/user/{uid}')
def get_user_info(uid: str = Path(...)):
    pass
```

Though you could hide the validation info of this path parameter, but people who have access to document already know there is a path parameter called `uid`.

I personally prefer your suggestion but felt it was better to be consistent with the rest of the file, e.g.

https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/cf8b080cf9392babefd905c166c1b7aa8145f1b9/fastapi/openapi/utils.py#L182

https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/blob/cf8b080cf9392babefd905c166c1b7aa8145f1b9/fastapi/openapi/utils.py#L320

I think this is ok as hiding any `Param` via `include_in_schema` is a user decision.

@yezz123 any thoughts on this?
Agreed @astraldawn, it's fine to leave it as is. I don't see why someone would want to hide the path param, but at the same time if they can hide the others, they should be able to hide this as well.
Thanks for the discussion! Yep, agreed it makes sense to switch it around. I just did in a commit on top. Thanks!

review_comments: You missed `Compatible`.

```suggestion
!!! note "참고"
```

```suggestion
Pydantic 모델과 같은 객체를 받고 JSON 호환 가능한 버전으로 반환합니다:
```
It's not a big deal; the original sentence used the term version, not data however.

It's good to keep the intention of the author as you can possible.

Again, for preserving the intention, don't break a sentence structure, please. 🤗

And I think "는" is proper than "은" where `jsonable_encoder` after.

```suggestion
길이가 긴 문자열 형태의 JSON 형식(문자열)의 데이터가 들어있는 상황에서는 `str`로 반환하지 않습니다. JSON과 모두 호환되는 값과 하위 값이 있는 Python 표준 데이터 구조 (예: `dict`)를 반환합니다.
```

```suggestion
실제로 `jsonable_encoder`는 **FastAPI** 에서 내부적으로 데이터를 변환하는 데 사용되지만, 다른 많은 곳에서도 유용하게 사용됩니다.
```

```suggestion
!!! 참고
```

```suggestion
JSON 호환 가능 데이터만 수신하는 `fake_db` 데이터베이스가 존재한다고 가정하겠습니다.
```

```suggestion
예를 들면, `datetime` 객체는 JSON과 호환되는 데이터가 아니므로 이 데이터는 받아들여지지 않습니다.
```

We have to write like:
```suggestion
!!! note "참고"
```

review_comments:
```suggestion
    Observa que debes pasar la petición como parte de las parejas clave-valor en el contexto de Jinja2. Por lo tanto, debes declararlo también en tus *operaciones de <abbr title="ruta">path</abbr>*.
```

```suggestion
    Declarando `response_class=HTMLResponse`, la documentación de la interfaz gráfica sabrá que la respuesta será un HTML.
```

```suggestion
Además, puedes usar `url_for()` dentro de una plantilla y utilizarlo, por ejemplo, con <abbr title="Archivos Estáticos">`StaticFiles`</abbr> que hayas montado.
```

```suggestion
Y como estas utilizando `StaticFiles`, ese archivo CSS será proporcionado automáticamente por tu aplicación de **FastAPI** en la URL `/static/styles.css`.
```

review_comments: I think "추가 모델" is better.

```suggestion
이전의 예제에 이어서 두 가지 이상의 관계 모델을 가지는 것이 일반적일 것입니다.
```

Don't break the original sentence structure, please. 😭

Sometimes, it's difficult to make it naturally with fixed sentence structure because Korean has not the same grammar as English.

Although, you should not break it as you can possible. We should
- preserve intentions of the author
- keep consistency for translations the others

I hope you accept my thought.

```suggestion
* **데이터베이스 모델**은 해시 된 비밀번호가 필요할 수 있습니다.
```
I think it's more natural to put "probably" to "~할 수 있습니다" rather than translate directly.
I'm not sure. Please tell me what you think.

Plus, you are using "비밀번호" in the docs, so you should use the same word.

```suggestion
    사용자의 평문 비밀번호를 절대 저장하지 마십시오. 항상 검증할 수 있는 "안전한 해시"로 저장하십시오.

    만약 모른다면 [보안 챕터](security/simple-oauth2.md#password-hashing){.internal-link target=_blank}에서 "비밀번호 해시"가 무엇인지 배울 수 있습니다.
```

Using "당신" is unnatural, right?
I think I'd better get rid of the expression "우리". thanks

```suggestion
이전의 예제에 이어서, 일반적으로는 한 가지 이상의 관계 모델을 가질 것입니다.

사용자 모델의 경우가 특히 그렇습니다. 왜냐하면:
```

title: add defaultModelsExpandDepth to config show depth or not show schema …
body: I add defaultModelsExpandDepth to provide config defaultModelsExpandDepth, cause in some case, I need to hide schema models and only show the endpoint. I set the default value to defaultModelsExpandDepth like on [swaggers documentation](https://swagger.io/docs/open-source-tools/swagger-ui/usage/configuration/).
comments: Can somebody please merge this?
@tiangolo  @Kludex  Any chance on this being merged soon?

I don't have merge rights. I'm also interested in this being merged. :smile: 

@tiangolo will eventually see this PR. :+1:
mark

I'm working on a PR for a generalised approach to this using a plug-in system. That way we won't end up with heaps of kwargs 

See - https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/4060

@rezandry, thank you for your efforts and for the interest in FastAPI!

Since [FastAPI 0.72.0](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/release-notes/#0720) you can specify Swagger UI parameters using `swagger_ui_parameters` argument to `FastAPI`.

```python
app = FastAPI(swagger_ui_parameters={"defaultModelsExpandDepth": -1})
```

I'll close this as resolved.
Thanks again!
review_comments:
```suggestion
    default_models_expand_depth: int = 1,
```

```suggestion
        defaultModelsExpandDepth: {default_models_expand_depth},
```

title: 🌐 Fix Korean translation for `docs/ko/docs/tutorial/first-steps.md`
body:
Hello, I carefully pulled a request on the already uploaded page. 
While I was quickly going through the docs to study, I found out a few trivial points to suggest. Please take a look if there's any part you like.

Here are what I suggested :
- corrected a trivial typo
- changed the tone that suit #2017 
- suggested some alternatives that seem to be closer to the author's original intent.

Plus, I have one thing to ask for your opinion. I personally think that putting the original terminoloy right after the translated word inside the parentheses seems to be a good way to reduce confusion for Korean readers. I am asking this because I saw some of your reviews on other PRs removing those English versions in parentheses, and still having the one within this file (동작(operation) <- this one).
If there's any translation guide on this matter, I would be very grateful if you let me know :)

Thanks always for your contributions and efforts!!

```suggestion
De la misma forma que puedes declarar parámetros con `Query`, `Path` y `Body` para realizar validaciones adicionales y de metadatos en la <abbr title="path operation">ruta de operacion</abbr> de una función, es posible realizar dichas acciones desde los modelos de Pydantic usando `Field`.
```
Hi there, I suggested to use the abbr tag for the translations

```suggestion
Observa cómo el atributo de cada modelo con un tipo, valor por defecto y `Field`, tiene la misma estructura que el parámetro de una función de <abbr title="path operation">operación de ruta</abbr>, con `Field` en lugar de `Path`, `Query` y `Body`.
```

```suggestion
También puedes usar los argumentos de <abbr title="keyword arguments">palabras clave</abbr> para pasar metadatos de esquema JSON adicionales.
```

```suggestion
## Importa `Field`

Primero, se tiene que importar:
```

It might be redundant to repeat the same text of the section title, the wording could be improved but that's what I could come up with as a suggestion.

```suggestion
    `Body` también devuelve objetos que son sublcase de `FieldInfo` directamente. Y hay otros que se verán más adelante que son subclases de la clase `Body`. 
```
Thanks @AdrianDeAnda for the suggestions

Is it "langues" like a language we speek, or language like a programming language?

I'm fixing this

body: In German, "eine gute Wahl" can be either "a good choice" or "a good election" but I don't think it works the other way around.
comments:
Ha! That's fun, yes, it's a mistranslation/thinking from Spanish. :sweat_smile: 

Thanks @oerpli! :cake:

title: Allow override of `BackgroundTasks` implementation.
body:
It would be nice to override the default implementation of `BackgroundTasks`.

My particular use case came about when looking for a way to easily push a number of background tasks to Celery in a fairly large code base. Splicing in a bunch of `return JSONResponse(background=...)` everywhere would be rather clunky.

This is a feature that has been asked for here:

* https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/805

And would be helpful for the use case here:

* https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/3133
* https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/3115
* https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1637

My approach was to look for `app.dependency_overrides[BackgroundTasks]` for a callable that would instantiate a `BackgroundTasks` instance. If it isn't overridden, we default the usual `BackgroundTasks` class.

Currently this is only overridable at the `FastAPI` app level and the constructor must not take any arguments.

Great, thank you @jaystone776 ! :tada: :cake: 

And thanks a lot for the reviews @voidf, @waynerv, and @Laineyzhang55 :rocket: :bow:
review_comments:
```suggestion
这段代码生成的 `dict` 只包含创建 `item` 模型时显式设置的数据，而不包括默认值。
```

```suggestion
然后再用它生成一个只含已设置（在请求中所发送）数据，且省略了默认值的 `dict`：
```

```suggestion
现在，你可以使用 `.copy()` 为已有的模型创建一个副本，并且传入 `update` 参数，参数值为包含将要更新数据的`dict`。
```

```suggestion
    因此，如果希望接收的部分更新数据可以省略其他所有属性，则要把模型中所有的属性标记为可选（使用默认值或 `None`）。
```

title: 🌐 Add Chinese translation for Tutorial - Request - Files
body: as title
comments:
Awesome, thank you @jaystone776 ! :rocket: :bow: 

And thanks @waynerv and @Laineyzhang55 for the reviews! :cake:
review_comments:
```suggestion
如果把*路径操作函数*参数的类型声明为 `bytes`，**FastAPI** 将以 `bytes` 形式读取和接收文件内容。
```
```suggestion * `file`： `SpooledTemporaryFile`（ 文件类对象）。其实就是 Python文件，可直接传递给其他预期 `file-like` 对象的函数或支持库。 ```

```suggestion
    可在一个*路径操作*中声明多个 `File` 和 `Form` 参数，但不能同时声明要接收 JSON 的 `Body` 字段。因为此时请求体的编码是 `multipart/form-data`，不是 `application/json`。
```

title: 🔧 Swap light/dark theme button icon
body:
Just a very minor PR for the FastAPI docs that swaps the light/dark theme icons around.  Currently the dark mode icon has the bulb filled (on) in the dark theme, and outlined (off) in the light theme.

Although not particularly important, most UI elements display the current state of affairs, rather than represent what will change when activated - for example, buttons are depressed when selected, not depressed prior to selection.  This PR simply aims to make the UX more consistent.
comments: Nice, thanks @eddsalkield! And thanks for taking the time to explain the argument. :nerd_face: :rocket:

title: 🌐 Add Spanish traslation for Tutorial - User Guide/Middleware
body:
Hi there :handshake:  !
This pull request include the Spanish translation of Tutorial - User Guide/middleware.md_es
This pull request continues with the Spanish translation of #1218
comments:
> Muy buena traducción, solo algunas recomendaciones
Muchas gracias por tus aportes Israel.
This was covered in another PR, so I'll close this one now, thanks for the effort! :cake: :coffee:
review_comments:
```suggestion
Un "middleware" es una función que se ejecuta con cada **petición** antes de que sea procesada por cualquier <abbr title="path operation">*operación de ruta*</abbr>. Y antes de devolver cada **respuesta**.
```

```suggestion
* Posteriormente, toma la **petición** para ser procesada por el resto de la aplicación (por alguna *operación de path*).
```

Hay algunos lugares en los que usamos `utilizar` por lo que estaría bien utilizar una palabra diferente con el mismo significado.
```suggestion
Para crear un middleware debemos usar el decorador `@app.middleware("http")` encima de la función.
```

```suggestion
    * Posteriormente, retorna la `respuesta` generada por la *operación de path*.
```

```suggestion
* Luego, puedes modificar aún más la `respuesta` antes de devolverla.
```

title: feat: add contributor over time graph to README
body:
Hi, community!

To better present how our community grows, we develop a tool to show contributors growing history on [https://github.com/api7/contributor-graph](https://github.com/api7/contributor-graph). Since we found it helpful, we think maybe if it could help some other community.

## WHAT IT IS

Basically, it just shows the contributors growth over time, just like the stargazers over time on the README. It would be the same with stars that we would update the graph each day, so the link would always present the real-time data. There is some other stuff to play around with if you would like to give it a try~

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/72343596/119220194-ed057100-bb1b-11eb-981b-8c956e886c65.png)

## HOW IT WORKS 

We use Github API to get all commits, try to find the “Github way” to filter commits so the result data would be similar to Github, and then get the first commit time of each user.

Don't hesitate to tell us if there is a better place to present this graph other than this, or there are some other worries or other features you would like to have~🍻
comments:
Hey, thanks for the suggestion, but I prefer to keep it as it is. If someone is interested in seeing a graph like that they can probably use your service directly or check out the contributors section: https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/graphs/contributors

But thanks for your interest! I'll close this now.

title: Fix: websocket route path not inherited  prefix value
body:
websocket route path not inherited  prefix value

In my case:
```
router = APIRouter(prefix = '/asset')
@router.websocket_route("/ws")
async def ws_func(ws: WebSocket):
    ....
app.include_router(router)
```
`ws_func` path should be  `/asset/ws` .
but `ws_func` path is `/ws` .
I found starlette WebSocketRoute not have prefix  attribute.

we need override add_websocket_route method for add router prefix .
comments: I guess that issue should also be solved by https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2640/

> I guess that issue should also be solved by #2640

Thank you for answer.

I mean, it was not merged, but it solves the issue :sweat_smile: 

@tiangolo needs to review it :kissing:
Hope to be release soon. :joy:

use `websocket` not `websocket_route`
try this:
```python
router = APIRouter(prefix = '/asset')
@router.websocket("/ws")
async def ws_func(ws: WebSocket):
    ....
app.include_router(router)
```

This works for me

title: add tags and path sorting, add related docs
body:
Hi, having the need to sort the swagger documentation, I made a quick changes that resolves : https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/issues/1000
It relies on https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui/issues/2990 for the underlying sort operation.
It might be a duplicate functionality regarding https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2337 but the way it works is way simpler as it relies heavily on swagger to do the work.
I also checked for Redoc ordering but reading https://github.com/Redocly/redoc/issues/98 it seems it is not done yet.
Please let me know if some changes are needed before you can merge, thanks for your work @tiangolo
comments: So I put together the doc changes we talked about and made a small implementation tweak to allow custom function execution along with the custom function example. I am not quite happy with this version cause it force people to use '"alpha"' instead of "alpha" (mind the quotes). Maybe we could do a test on whether it's alpha/method and put quotes in those cases; although this would imply updating tests if SwaggerUI adds more ordering.
@tiangolo can I hope this to be merged anytime soon ? Or is there anything to change in the proposed code, or the proposed doc update ?

Hi, this functionality has been implemented as part of [#2568 ](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi/pull/2568 ) under the *swagger_ui_parameters* so it is no longer needed, I'm closing to keep thing clean, thank you all
review_comments:
We already have docs about tags here: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/path-operation-configuration/?h=tags#tags and here https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/?h=tags#use-your-tags
So don't see a use for this?
Is there a doc where all of them are listed? Dislike the fact it's linking to an issue, couldn't figure out the complete list myself.
A example method for "sort" would be 👍

Well I must have missed the second one, and the first one uses a slightly different way of defining tags; 
Seeing this, I think the part about Tags sorting could be added to https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/?h=tags#order-of-tags which is immediately after the second link you gave.

Also in the extending openapi doc we need to have tags defined so maybe we can just skip the Add Tags and instead put a link toward the doc here https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-tags

Let me know what you think so I can rewrite this part of the doc.

There is a doc here https://swagger.io/docs/open-source-tools/swagger-ui/usage/configuration/ but it is very general and options are listed along a lot of other. Maybe it is possible to put direct link or highlight...

The Swagger UI link back to https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/sort
but yes I think I could manage to create a custom sort function as an example. Something like elt1.length> elt2.length could be kinda visual I think
Doesn't need to be *that* visual, just changing around 1 endpoint would be enough.
Considering it's not in a separate table I doubt you could make a good highlight, think it's still better link than github issue, just need a way to point to the correct variables
I like adding it to https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/?h=tags#order-of-tags, the https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-tags seems like a different thing?

Hi, I included in the doc (metadata/order of tags) a link to the extending-openapi doc. metadata for tags is a bit different as it is the customization of the tags headers with markdown description and url link
I included the link to this general swagger doc and put as a technical note the link to the issue describing the technical implementation

I wrote a function in a generic manner using the length as ordering as it is simple to understand, effectively changes the display order in a easy to check way and as a bonus it allows to show the way of getting each entry "name" which is the operation path.

```
     operations_sorter='function(e,t) {\
            if (e.get("path").length < t.get("path").length) {return -1;}\
            if (e.get("path").length > t.get("path").length) {return 1;}\
            return 0;\
            }',
```

title: 🛠 Native JWT support
body:
### Relevance
About half of the modern internet uses raw JWT (the other half uses OAuth2, which is already supported).  
I think that in such a modern library as `fastapi`, native support of JWT will be a big advantage.

### Existed solutions 
Only 1 external lib: [github:fastapi-jwt-auth (192 stars)](https://github.com/IndominusByte/fastapi-jwt-auth/) - an flask-jwt-auth customization. 

The following drawbacks are significant:
1. poorly supported (last updated in 2020)
2. depends on the original library
3. DOES NOT match the `fastapi` security style (`Security`/`Depends` function args wrapper)
4. no `openapi` specification  generation

### Existed Issues and Pull Requests:
* Searching 'jwt' or 'json web tokens' - 'No results matched' (except docs example)

---

### Features 

- [x] bearer/cookie token place
- [x] access/refresh tokens 
- [x] time expiring tokens 
- [x] `auto_error=False` support
- [x] `openapi` specification generation
- [ ] fresh token demand
- [ ] token revocation
- [ ] cookies name selecting
- [ ] docs examples (after approval)


### Requrements 
`python-jose` for tokens interaction and `pytest-mock` for testing time-dependent functions.
 

### Usage example 
All available use cases can be found in the test files. The simplest example is provided below:

```python
app = FastAPI()

access_security = JwtAccessBearer(secret_key="secret_key")

@app.post("/auth")
def auth():
    subject = {"username": "username", "role": "user"}
    access_token = access_security.create_access_token(subject=subject)
    return {"access_token": access_token, "refresh_token": None}

@app.get("/users/me")
def read_current_user(credentials: JwtAuthorizationCredentials = Security(access_security)):
    return {"username": credentials["username"], "role": credentials["role"]}
```

---

Any suggestions for improvement are gladly discussed!
comments: @k4black  in security docs @tiangolo  in the past used  Pyjwt but later he moved to [python-jose](https://github.com/mpdavis/python-jose)  PR  [#1610] so I think we should consider using python-jose for your pull request 🤔
@tiangolo Can you approve or decline this PR, please
Is this going to be approved or has this just been ignored? Seems like a nice, easy way to add authentication with the option of refresh tokens to me.

Is there any chance that this PR or comparable functionality will be merged soon?
What are the next steps here @tiangolo @cikay @k4black @acutaia , I might be willing to take over some work.
Would be very handy to have jwt support out of the box!
@tiangolo, any update?
python-jose is unmaintained. Please do not use it.

> python-jose is unmaintained. Please do not use it.

latest version of fastapi-jwt allows to use authlib instead of python-jose (still present as fallback)
Any update?

Thanks for the interest @k4black! 

So, JWT and OAuth2 are two separate things. OAuth2 is about the exchange of communication via HTTP to handle auth stuff and exchange of tokens, without specifying any format for the tokens.

JWT is a format for the tokens. Using OAuth2 it's quite common to exchange JWT tokens.

"Bearer" is a header format to transfer tokens. Normally, the 3 go together.

For example, if you check the security docs, they are about using Bearer tokens in JWT format using OAuth2.

Parsing and handling tokens is not really dependent of any web stuff, so I think it would be better in an external package.

This also adds a strong dependency on python-jose that I wouldn't want to take right now.

For now, I'll pass on this one, but thanks for the interest! :cake:
review_comments: Do not reverse the condition add the raise keyword to the if block.
This is where you should reverse the condition the success case should be at the bottom. [It is called early return or fast fail.](https://szymonkrajewski.pl/why-should-you-return-early/)

review_comments:
```suggestion
# Implementação - Introdução
```

```suggestion
A implementação de uma aplicação **FastAPI** é relativamente simples.
```

It feels redundant 🤔
```suggestion
Existem várias maneiras para fazer isso, dependendo do seu caso e das ferramentas que você utiliza.
```
I think `Deploy` is often translated as `implantação`.
Maybe `Existem várias maneiras para fazer isso, dependendo do seu caso específico e das ferramentas que você utiliza.` ?
Sure :+1:

I've seen `Deploy` translated as `implantação` and `Implementação`, but I believe that the first one makes more sense because we're inserting it in the cloud. 

I believe that `Implementação` would be a synonym to `implantação` in our context.

I will leave here some resources that helped me to pick `implantação`:
[Comparative between the two words](https://www.sinonimos.com.br/implantar/)
[Implantar synonyms/meaning](https://www.sinonimos.com.br/implantar/)
[implementar synonyms/meaning](https://www.sinonimos.com.br/implementar/)

Let me know what you guys think and if I should keep it or switch to `Implementação`.

Eu acho que ficaria mais completo se fosse adicionado `caso de uso especifico`.
```suggestion
Existem várias maneiras para fazer isso, dependendo do seu caso de uso específico e das ferramentas que você utiliza.
```
In a previous discussion, we decided to remove the `use case`, because it sounded redundant. `use case` is already `specific`, hence they can't be together we decided to stay with only specific
Ok, that's a really good point that I didn't thinked of !!!

title: ✏ Re-word and combine 2 paragraphs into 1 for readability
body:
👋 Hi everyone! 

This is a very little change that I think it improves the readability of the introduction text (even though I think we don't even need the last 2 paragraphs of the introduction, because the second paragraph already says the point of the others 2)

### Reasons of the change
- **You can read the first 3 paragraphs independently** and they give you a good idea about what each of them is talking about.
- But **with the last paragraph you can't do that** in my opinion. Because it is tightly related with the paragraph 3. That's why I combined paragraph 3 and 4.
- I removed the word `diseñado` from paragraph 3, because I think we don't actually need it. Paragraph 2 already says how the Tutorial is designed (gradually...) and the main focus of paragraph 3 is the functionally the Tutorial may have in the future for the user.

**Old version:**
```
Este tutorial te muestra cómo usar FastAPI con la mayoría de sus características paso a paso.

Cada sección se basa gradualmente en las anteriores, pero está estructurada en temas separados, así puedes ir directamente a cualquier tema en concreto para resolver tus necesidades específicas sobre la API.

También está diseñado para funcionar como una referencia futura.

Para que puedas volver y ver exactamente lo que necesitas.
```

**2 last paragraphs combined**
```
Funciona también como una referencia futura, para que puedas volver y ver exactamente lo que necesitas.
```

Please let me know what you think 🙏
comments: Yep, that makes sense. Thank you @jalvaradosegura! :coffee: :cake:

title: ⬆ Upgrade internal testing dependencies: mypy to version 0.910, add newly needed type packages
body:
Mypy has released a new version which includes a new nice features.

This PR includes:

1. Update `mypy` dependency to 0.901 from 0.812
2. Addition of 'types' dependencies due to update to Typeshed (http://mypy-lang.blogspot.com/2021/05/the-upcoming-switch-to-modular-typeshed.html)
3. Removal of mypy.ini and inclusion of it inside of pyproject.toml.

---

**Edit** (by @tiangolo): I upgraded `mypy` and the new stubs to their current latest. I also added `types-dataclasses` that is needed for Python 3.6.
comments: Should probably update this to 0.910 now?

title: Use Decimal for certain OpenAPI params to avoid losing precision
body:
Currently, the schema parameters `multipleOf`, `maximum`, `exclusiveMaximum`, `minimum`, and `exclusiveMinimum` are forcibly cast to `float`.

This causes a couple problems with OpenAPI:
* From [the docs on `multipleOf`](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/data-models/data-types/#multipleOf): 
  > `multipleOf` may be used with floating-point numbers, but in practice this can be unreliable due to the limited precision or floating point math.
* It loses precision with large numbers. For example, if we wanted to have a field that accepts a 64-bit integer (in the range `-9223372036854775808` to `9223372036854775807`), casting that to float converts it to +/-`9.223372036854776e+18`, resulting in +/-`9223372036854776000` when cast back to int. This causes a significant difference between what the developer intends to be a valid value, and what actually gets validated against (and displayed in error messages).

Declaring those fields as `Decimal` avoids losing precision through unwanted `float` conversions.
comments:
I'm seeing that this would only work to override the generated OpenAPI, but it wouldn't alter the internal implementation in Pydantic and the actual validation, right?

In that case, I feel it would be dangerous to make it look like some validation is done, but it's actually no exactly that. :thinking:
📝 Docs preview for commit 3499d5fdf73f2eb0621ffff914021e158ea4ae4f at: https://631086a5fdaaf7547154ac9d--fastapi.netlify.app
As this PR has been waiting for the original user for a while but seems to be inactive, it's now going to be closed. But if there's anyone interested, feel free to create a new PR.

title: Add default for `default` argument in Path
body:
This pull request means that the following the code from [Path Parameters and Numeric Validations - FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/path-params-numeric-validations/#declare-metadata) can omit its pointless default argument:
```py
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, Path, Query

app = FastAPI()


@app.get("/items/{item_id}")
async def read_items(
    # item_id: int = Path(..., title="The ID of the item to get"),
    item_id: int = Path(title="The ID of the item to get"),
    q: Optional[str] = Query(None, alias="item-query"),
):
    results = {"item_id": item_id}
    if q:
        results.update({"q": q})
    return results
```

As explained by the note under it, and by looking at the source, we can make out that this argument never had any meaning.
> A path parameter is always required as it has to be part of the path.
> 
> So, you should declare it with `...` to mark it as required.
> 
> Nevertheless, even if you declared it with None or set a default value, it would not affect anything, it would still be always required.

Looking at the source (simplified as to not take up too much space):
```py
class Path(Param):
    def __init__(
        self,
        default: Any,
        *,
        **extra: Any,
    ):
        super().__init__(
            ...,
            **extra,
        )
```
comments: I have now also edited the documentation.
I have now rebased this and squashed the commits into a more fitting commit message.

Can't we completely remove the `default` parameter from the `Path` object's signature? It is a bit misleading/confusing as it is. Path has no default value, period. As it currently stands, VS Code will (rightfully) tell me I can supply a default value for `Path`, which brings up all kinds of questions about its behaviour.

I wanted to create an issue/PR for that but I see you've already addressed most of it.

> Can't we completely remove the `default` parameter from the `Path` object's signature? It is a bit misleading/confusing as it is. Path has no default value, period.

I believe the reason it is there is because it has to implement implement and replicate the Param class types, which does have a `default` parameter.

It is also a breaking change to remove now, what would be your suggestion?

I think that's the reason too. But adding a nonsensical arg is maybe even worse than not having the signatures match (apparently we're trying to force things to act the same that are not the same). I'm not sure if internally the code would stop working if we get rid of it though.

It is for sure a breaking change.. No way around it. After thinking about it for a bit more I think your solution is the best way to make this work easier without breaking anything.

~~A slightly more breaking way is to replace the name `default` with anything indicating that `default` is not used at all. Even `_` would be good. I think a lot of people will have used `Path` as `Path(...)` so that won't break. However, if people have used it as `Path(default=...)` then it _will_ break.~~
Actually, we can change the name of `default` to `_` and make sure that we pop `default` from the `extra` kwargs to handle the situation where they've actually passed the default param as `Path(default=...)`. 

Even more breaking would be to remove the parameter altogether.

Perhaps this could start from your solution and work its way up to complete removal, while updating the docs and maybe emitting `DeprecationWarning`s

Although we are still in the 0.y.z phase, I agree that we shouldn't take breaking backwards compatibility lightly.

Thanks for the effort @Bluenix2! :cake: 

And thanks for the conversation and discussion @ArcLightSlavik and @frerikandriessen. :coffee: 

This was in another PR, now no parameter needs a default value unless one is actually defined. If it's not defined, it's taken as required. This works for `Query`, `Path`, `Cookie`, etc.

Given that, I'll close this PR now, but thanks for the interest! :cookie:
review_comments: Maybe just leave out the "You should declare it with ..." part?

It still has meaning, we're explaining that compared to Query parameters, Path parameters are always required and that's why you should declare it with `...` to mark it as such (in relation to Query parameters). Then adding that since this is the case, for Path parameters FastAPI defaults to `...` if you leave it out.

title: ✨ Enable Pydantic's serialization mode for responses, add support for Pydantic's `computed_field`, better OpenAPI for response models, proper required attributes, better generated clients
body:
✨ Enable Pydantic's serialization mode for responses, add support for Pydantic's `computed_field`.

Ref: https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/usage/computed_fields/
comments:
@tiangolo This change seems to break parts of OpenAPI / JSONSchema generation.

Specifically, fields that should not be "required" in the schema now are.

Simple repro:

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI()


class Item(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    is_offer: None | bool = None


@app.get("/item", response_model=Item)
async def get_item():
    return {
        "name": "Foo",
        "price": 42.0,
        "is_offer": True,
    }
```

In the above, the generated schema for the `Item` model has `is_offer` listed in the `required` field of the JSON schema, even though the [docs for Pydantic 2](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/migration/#required-optional-and-nullable-fields) indicate that this should *not* be required, and it was working as expected in FastAPI 0.100.x

Can make a separate issue for this, but thought I'd post here first.
